1	# Arlindo — Handoff for the next session
2	
3	> **Read this first.** It hands off from the cloud sessions that built Arlindo's IUL product
4	> to a fresh local session that will (1) research National Life **Term** products into the
5	> knowledge base, (2) build a **Term** product type using the real sample illustration, and
6	> (3) polish the UI/presentation. Also read `CLAUDE.md` (architecture + conventions) and
7	> `.claude/rules/arlindo-wave-ownership.md` (seam map — note the "frozen" rules were for the
8	> original parallel build; a single session may edit across seams, but keep the architecture).
9	
10	## What Arlindo is
11	A free, client-side web app for an older insurance agent (Second Chance Financial · National
12	Life Group, Brazil) to type carrier-illustration numbers and generate a polished client
13	presentation to present live and export as **PDF** and **PowerPoint (.pptx)**. UI is **pt-BR**;
14	the generated presentation can be **PT / EN / ES**. Top product today: **IUL (FlexLife)**.
15	
16	## Current state (live on `main`)
17	Deployed via GitHub Pages at **https://moronisalvador.github.io/Arlindo/** (deploy runs from
18	`main` only — `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`). Shipped and working end-to-end for **IUL**:
19	- Presentations home + CRUD + duplicate + JSON backup; agent profile settings.
20	- Data-entry: collapsible sections, source toggle (typed illustration vs app estimate) at the
21	  top, per-mode fields, auto-derived values (living benefit, projected value, table Idade/DB),
22	  rider editor (accessible %: 80/90/100 presets + custom; 90% kept per the rep's real usage).
23	- Present mode (keyboard/swipe/wake-lock), PDF export (print CSS), **editable .pptx** (pptxgenjs).
24	- App-computed **estimator** at `/calculo` (`IulProjectionEngine`, approximate, AG 49-B-capped,
25	  labeled "estimativa — não garantida").
26	- **Multi-language output** PT/EN/ES (`src/domain/presentationCopy.ts` + `localeFor`).
27	- **Real SCF logo** (`public/scf-logo.png` via `BrandLogo`); navy + gold brand.
28	- **Knowledge base** `docs/knowledge/` — FlexLife facts (confidence + audit verdict + sources),
29	  the raw adversarial audit JSON, and a dormant estimator-calibration guide.
30	
31	## Commands
32	`npm run dev` · `npm run build` (typecheck + build) · `npm test` (vitest, 19 passing) ·
33	`npm run preview`. Chromium/Playwright preinstalled for screenshots (see CLAUDE.md env notes).
34	
35	## Architecture map (key files)
36	- **Model (zod source of truth):** `src/domain/model/presentation.ts` (`PresentationInputs`,
37	  `productType` = `'iul' | 'annuity'` today — **add `'term'`**), `derived.ts` (renderer contract),
38	  `products.ts` (NLG product registry), `riders.ts` (ABR suite + disclaimers).
39	- **Calc engines:** `src/domain/calc/` — `PassthroughEngine` (typed numbers), `IulProjectionEngine`
40	  (estimate), `index.ts` `selectEngine`/`derive`.
41	- **Presentation copy (PT/EN/ES) + rider/disclaimer translations:** `src/domain/presentationCopy.ts`.
42	- **Formatting:** `src/domain/format/index.ts` (`formatMoney/Number/Percent`, `localeFor`).
43	- **Slides:** `src/design-system/slides/iul/*` + `buildSlides.tsx`; fixed 16:9 stage; `renderMode`
44	  preview/present/print. Features import ONLY from `@design-system`.
45	- **Features:** `src/features/{presentations,data-entry,present,export-pdf,calc-coming-soon,settings}`.
46	- **Export:** `src/export/pptx.ts`. **Routing:** `src/app/routes.ts`, `router.tsx`.
47	- **Sample illustration (Term):** `Dave Miller.pdf` — a **Term 30-G / LSW** illustration
48	  ($600k, $71.54/mo, Male 39 Elite Non-Tobacco, VA; 5 ABRs; conversion privilege). **Git-ignored**
49	  (contains client PII) — keep your local copy in the repo folder; it won't be committed.
50	
51	---
52	
53	## Next work
54	
55	### 1) Research National Life **Term** products → knowledge base (do this FIRST)
56	Replicate exactly the method used for IUL so the Term KB matches:
57	- Run the **`deep-research`** skill on NLG/LSW **Term** life products (lineup, e.g. Term 30-G and
58	  the rest of the term series; issue ages/lengths; conversion privilege terms; the ABR suite on
59	  term; premium structure; state variations; foreign-national rules).
60	- Then run an **adversarial accuracy audit** (an ultracode `Workflow`, mirroring the IUL run):
61	  per claim, one evidence-gathering verifier + one skeptic prompted to refute, against NLG/LSW
62	  primary sources; synthesize.
63	- Produce, mirroring `docs/knowledge/national-life-flexlife.md`:
64	  - `docs/knowledge/national-life-term.md` — curated facts, each tagged **confidence + verdict +
65	    source URLs**, with a corrections/decisions section.
66	  - `docs/knowledge/term-audit-<date>.json` — the raw verdicts + synthesis, verbatim.
67	  - Update `docs/knowledge/README.md` to list the Term files.
68	- **Cross-check against `Dave Miller.pdf`** (real Term 30-G): ledger is **Policy Year · Age ·
69	  Guaranteed Premium · Guaranteed Death Benefit** only — **no cash value / CSV / accumulation /
70	  LIBR** (term has none). ABR living-benefit values are **discounted, condition-specific** (sample:
71	  Terminal ~86%, Critical/Injury ~79%, Alzheimer's ~62% of the $600k DB) — not a flat %.
72	
73	### 2) Build the **Term** product type (use the sample as the reference)
74	- Add `productType: 'term'` to `productTypeSchema`; add a `term` fields object (face/death benefit,
75	  premium + mode, term length, conversion window, ABR riders, and a level premium schedule) to the
76	  zod model — additive, keep `schemaVersion`/migrations sane.
77	- Add Term product(s) to the `NLG_PRODUCTS` registry (carrier LSW/NL, min face, positioning,
78	  `available: true`); only ship **guaranteed/structural** facts — never hardcode current rates.
79	- **Term slide set** (branch `buildSlides` on `productType`): reuse Cover, the
80	  Protection/Living-benefits headline (**drop the accumulation card** — term has no cash value),
81	  a Coverage slide (premium + death benefit + living benefit + conversion privilege), a
82	  **Premium Schedule** table (Policy Year · Age · Premium · Death Benefit), the ABR detail, a
83	  **Term-vs-Permanent** comparison, and Disclaimers. **Drop** the IUL Projection (accumulated-value
84	  growth), the accumulated-value year table, and the Withdraw-vs-Income "Duas Opções" slides — none
85	  apply to term.
86	- Term has **no estimator** (no cash value); the data-entry "Estimar no app" toggle should not
87	  appear for term.
88	- Localize all new Term copy in PT/EN/ES via `presentationCopy.ts`; add per-feature i18n strings.
89	- Verify with a generated Term deck (screenshot present + PDF + a .pptx) against `Dave Miller.pdf`.
90	
91	### 3) UI / presentation polish
92	- **Mobile/iPhone** (deferred): header/nav overflow at 390px; present-mode portrait "rotate" hint.
93	  Test at 390px + iPad.
94	- General presentation polish (spacing, type scale, slide density) — use the design system tokens
95	  (`src/design-system/tokens/`), keep navy + gold, high contrast, large touch targets.
96	
97	## Guardrails (carry over)
98	- **Never hardcode** current caps/participation/bonus/illustrated rates — rate-sheet-driven; only
99	  guaranteed minimums + structural facts are safe. The **official carrier illustration is
100	  authoritative**; app estimates are labeled "estimativa — não garantida".
101	- **Compliance:** required disclaimers auto-render; foreign-national/Brazil = USD, min face ~$500k,
102	  US solicitation/illustration/signing/delivery; ABR access generally requires the client in the US.
103	- **pt-BR UI**; all strings via i18n; currency USD via `@domain/format`.
104	- **Local-first** (IndexedDB source of truth); do nothing on resume/focus; render loading/empty/error.
105	- **Deploy from `main` only.** Develop on a feature branch; open a **draft PR**; the human merges to
106	  deploy. Keep model identifiers out of commits/PRs/code.
107	
108	## Dormant / later
109	- **Estimator calibration (Path B):** `docs/knowledge/calibration-capture-list.md` — needs a real
110	  **FlexLife IUL** illustration (the Dave Miller sample is Term, no cash-value ledger to fit).
111	- **Supabase sync:** local-first works today; flip on `PassthroughSync` once the project is reachable
112	  (run `supabase/migrations/0001_init.sql` once).
113	- **Annuity** + the other NLG products via the same registry.
114	
115	## ⚠️ Privacy note (`Dave Miller.pdf`)
116	Now **git-ignored** (`.gitignore`: `Dave Miller.pdf`, `/samples/`, `*.illustration.pdf`) — keep client
117	illustrations local, never commit them to this public repo. **Note:** the file still exists in earlier
118	git history (it was committed before being ignored); if "Dave Miller" is a real client and that matters,
119	scrub it from history (`git filter-repo`/BFG + force-push) — ask the human first, since it rewrites `main`.
120	
121	## Verification checklist for the next session
122	- `npm run build` clean; `npm test` green.
123	- Term: create a Term presentation from `Dave Miller.pdf`'s numbers → present + PDF + .pptx match;
124	  no IUL-only slides appear; PT/EN/ES all render.
125	- Term KB: only high-confidence verified facts marked "product-accurate"; sources cited.
126	- Screenshot key screens at desktop + 390px before opening the PR.

# Arlindo — Handoff for the next session

> **Read this first.** It hands off from the cloud sessions that built Arlindo's IUL product
> to a fresh local session that will (1) research National Life **Term** products into the
> knowledge base, (2) build a **Term** product type using the real sample illustration, and
> (3) polish the UI/presentation. Also read `CLAUDE.md` (architecture + conventions) and
> `.claude/rules/arlindo-wave-ownership.md` (seam map — note the "frozen" rules were for the
> original parallel build; a single session may edit across seams, but keep the architecture).

## What Arlindo is
A free, client-side web app for an older insurance agent (Second Chance Financial · National
Life Group, Brazil) to type carrier-illustration numbers and generate a polished client
presentation to present live and export as **PDF** and **PowerPoint (.pptx)**. UI is **pt-BR**;
the generated presentation can be **PT / EN / ES**. Top product today: **IUL (FlexLife)**.

## Current state (live on `main`)
Deployed via GitHub Pages at **https://moronisalvador.github.io/Arlindo/** (deploy runs from
`main` only — `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`). Shipped and working end-to-end for **IUL**:
- Presentations home + CRUD + duplicate + JSON backup; agent profile settings.
- Data-entry: collapsible sections, source toggle (typed illustration vs app estimate) at the
  top, per-mode fields, auto-derived values (living benefit, projected value, table Idade/DB),
  rider editor (accessible %: 80/90/100 presets + custom; 90% kept per the rep's real usage).
- Present mode (keyboard/swipe/wake-lock), PDF export (print CSS), **editable .pptx** (pptxgenjs).
- App-computed **estimator** at `/calculo` (`IulProjectionEngine`, approximate, AG 49-B-capped,
  labeled "estimativa — não garantida").
- **Multi-language output** PT/EN/ES (`src/domain/presentationCopy.ts` + `localeFor`).
- **Real SCF logo** (`public/scf-logo.png` via `BrandLogo`); navy + gold brand.
- **Knowledge base** `docs/knowledge/` — FlexLife facts (confidence + audit verdict + sources),
  the raw adversarial audit JSON, and a dormant estimator-calibration guide.

## Commands
`npm run dev` · `npm run build` (typecheck + build) · `npm test` (vitest, 19 passing) ·
`npm run preview`. Chromium/Playwright preinstalled for screenshots (see CLAUDE.md env notes).

## Architecture map (key files)
- **Model (zod source of truth):** `src/domain/model/presentation.ts` (`PresentationInputs`,
  `productType` = `'iul' | 'annuity'` today — **add `'term'`**), `derived.ts` (renderer contract),
  `products.ts` (NLG product registry), `riders.ts` (ABR suite + disclaimers).
- **Calc engines:** `src/domain/calc/` — `PassthroughEngine` (typed numbers), `IulProjectionEngine`
  (estimate), `index.ts` `selectEngine`/`derive`.
- **Presentation copy (PT/EN/ES) + rider/disclaimer translations:** `src/domain/presentationCopy.ts`.
- **Formatting:** `src/domain/format/index.ts` (`formatMoney/Number/Percent`, `localeFor`).
- **Slides:** `src/design-system/slides/iul/*` + `buildSlides.tsx`; fixed 16:9 stage; `renderMode`
  preview/present/print. Features import ONLY from `@design-system`.
- **Features:** `src/features/{presentations,data-entry,present,export-pdf,calc-coming-soon,settings}`.
- **Export:** `src/export/pptx.ts`. **Routing:** `src/app/routes.ts`, `router.tsx`.
- **Sample illustration (Term):** `Dave Miller.pdf` — a **Term 30-G / LSW** illustration
  ($600k, $71.54/mo, Male 39 Elite Non-Tobacco, VA; 5 ABRs; conversion privilege). **Git-ignored**
  (contains client PII) — keep your local copy in the repo folder; it won't be committed.

---

## Next work

### 1) Research National Life **Term** products → knowledge base (do this FIRST)
Replicate exactly the method used for IUL so the Term KB matches:
- Run the **`deep-research`** skill on NLG/LSW **Term** life products (lineup, e.g. Term 30-G and
  the rest of the term series; issue ages/lengths; conversion privilege terms; the ABR suite on
  term; premium structure; state variations; foreign-national rules).
- Then run an **adversarial accuracy audit** (an ultracode `Workflow`, mirroring the IUL run):
  per claim, one evidence-gathering verifier + one skeptic prompted to refute, against NLG/LSW
  primary sources; synthesize.
- Produce, mirroring `docs/knowledge/national-life-flexlife.md`:
  - `docs/knowledge/national-life-term.md` — curated facts, each tagged **confidence + verdict +
    source URLs**, with a corrections/decisions section.
  - `docs/knowledge/term-audit-<date>.json` — the raw verdicts + synthesis, verbatim.
  - Update `docs/knowledge/README.md` to list the Term files.
- **Cross-check against `Dave Miller.pdf`** (real Term 30-G): ledger is **Policy Year · Age ·
  Guaranteed Premium · Guaranteed Death Benefit** only — **no cash value / CSV / accumulation /
  LIBR** (term has none). ABR living-benefit values are **discounted, condition-specific** (sample:
  Terminal ~86%, Critical/Injury ~79%, Alzheimer's ~62% of the $600k DB) — not a flat %.

### 2) Build the **Term** product type (use the sample as the reference)
- Add `productType: 'term'` to `productTypeSchema`; add a `term` fields object (face/death benefit,
  premium + mode, term length, conversion window, ABR riders, and a level premium schedule) to the
  zod model — additive, keep `schemaVersion`/migrations sane.
- Add Term product(s) to the `NLG_PRODUCTS` registry (carrier LSW/NL, min face, positioning,
  `available: true`); only ship **guaranteed/structural** facts — never hardcode current rates.
- **Term slide set** (branch `buildSlides` on `productType`): reuse Cover, the
  Protection/Living-benefits headline (**drop the accumulation card** — term has no cash value),
  a Coverage slide (premium + death benefit + living benefit + conversion privilege), a
  **Premium Schedule** table (Policy Year · Age · Premium · Death Benefit), the ABR detail, a
  **Term-vs-Permanent** comparison, and Disclaimers. **Drop** the IUL Projection (accumulated-value
  growth), the accumulated-value year table, and the Withdraw-vs-Income "Duas Opções" slides — none
  apply to term.
- Term has **no estimator** (no cash value); the data-entry "Estimar no app" toggle should not
  appear for term.
- Localize all new Term copy in PT/EN/ES via `presentationCopy.ts`; add per-feature i18n strings.
- Verify with a generated Term deck (screenshot present + PDF + a .pptx) against `Dave Miller.pdf`.

### 3) UI / presentation polish
- **Mobile/iPhone** (deferred): header/nav overflow at 390px; present-mode portrait "rotate" hint.
  Test at 390px + iPad.
- General presentation polish (spacing, type scale, slide density) — use the design system tokens
  (`src/design-system/tokens/`), keep navy + gold, high contrast, large touch targets.

## Guardrails (carry over)
- **Never hardcode** current caps/participation/bonus/illustrated rates — rate-sheet-driven; only
  guaranteed minimums + structural facts are safe. The **official carrier illustration is
  authoritative**; app estimates are labeled "estimativa — não garantida".
- **Compliance:** required disclaimers auto-render; foreign-national/Brazil = USD, min face ~$500k,
  US solicitation/illustration/signing/delivery; ABR access generally requires the client in the US.
- **pt-BR UI**; all strings via i18n; currency USD via `@domain/format`.
- **Local-first** (IndexedDB source of truth); do nothing on resume/focus; render loading/empty/error.
- **Deploy from `main` only.** Develop on a feature branch; open a **draft PR**; the human merges to
  deploy. Keep model identifiers out of commits/PRs/code.

## Dormant / later
- **Estimator calibration (Path B):** `docs/knowledge/calibration-capture-list.md` — needs a real
  **FlexLife IUL** illustration (the Dave Miller sample is Term, no cash-value ledger to fit).
- **Supabase sync:** local-first works today; flip on `PassthroughSync` once the project is reachable
  (run `supabase/migrations/0001_init.sql` once).
- **Annuity** + the other NLG products via the same registry.

## ⚠️ Privacy note (`Dave Miller.pdf`)
Now **git-ignored** (`.gitignore`: `Dave Miller.pdf`, `/samples/`, `*.illustration.pdf`) — keep client
illustrations local, never commit them to this public repo. **Note:** the file still exists in earlier
git history (it was committed before being ignored); if "Dave Miller" is a real client and that matters,
scrub it from history (`git filter-repo`/BFG + force-push) — ask the human first, since it rewrites `main`.

## Verification checklist for the next session
- `npm run build` clean; `npm test` green.
- Term: create a Term presentation from `Dave Miller.pdf`'s numbers → present + PDF + .pptx match;
  no IUL-only slides appear; PT/EN/ES all render.
- Term KB: only high-confidence verified facts marked "product-accurate"; sources cited.
- Screenshot key screens at desktop + 390px before opening the PR.

The handoff clarifies that the frozen rules applied to the original parallel build, but a single session can edit across different sections while maintaining the architecture — that unblocks the next phases. The PDF is already gitignored due to PII concerns, so I won't re-commit it.

content.subject: Baseline setup: install, build, test
content.description: npm install, confirm npm run build passes and npm test (19 tests) pass on clean main before any work.

content.subject: Phase 1: NLG Term knowledge base + adversarial audit
content.description: deep-research NLG/LSW Term life; ultracode Workflow adversarial audit (per-claim verifier + skeptic vs primary sources + synthesis); produce docs/knowledge/national-life-term.md (confidence+verdict+URLs), docs/knowledge/term-audit-<date>.json (raw), update knowledge README. Feature branch + draft PR.

content.subject: Phase 2: Build Term product type
content.description: Using Dave Miller.pdf (Term 30-G) as reference: add productType 'term', Term fields, Term registry entry, Term-specific slide set (drop IUL accumulation/projection/withdraw; add premium-schedule table + conversion + ABR + term-vs-permanent). Localize PT/EN/ES. Verify Term deck present+PDF+.pptx matches sample. Feature branch + draft PR.

content.subject: Phase 3: UI + presentation polish (incl. mobile)
content.description: Polish UI + presentation including mobile: 390px header/nav overflow, present-mode portrait hint. Feature branch + draft PR.

235	The optional Accelerated Benefit Riders (ABR) offer you flexibility to access your death benefit if you

243	                           Age 43 $4,426                                                 Age 43 $343,715

251	                                                  At age 45, between $0 and $474,703.
252	                                                  At age 49, between $0 and $474,549.
253	
254	                                                        What does this mean for me and my family?
255	
256	                          If I become ill, there is the ability to access a portion of the death benefit NOW. This will help
257	                                 me and my family maintain our lifestyle...even during a challenging financial time.
258	
259	                                 Depending on the severity of illness or injury, the ABR benefit, if available, will be
260	                           determined at time of claim. Based on impact on life expectancy, the benefit amount could
261	
262	                                                                                   be $0.
263	
264	The critical illness or injury amount, if any, will be determined using four different levels depending on the severity of the
265	illness or injury. Many illnesses actually have a minimal impact on future mortality which could mean a small or even no
266	benefit even with a qualifying event. At time of claim, we will assess mortality and other factors to determine the benefit
267	amount that may be payable. The owner has the option to decline to make a claim after receiving a benefit estimate.
268	
269	You can accelerate up to 100% of the death benefit, subject to an ABR Benefit limit of $1,500,000 for terminal illness, an
270	ABR Benefit limit of $1,500,000 for chronic illness, an ABR Benefit limit of $1,000,000 for critical illness and critical injury
271	and an ABR Benefit limit of $1,500,000 for alzheimer's disease on the total death benefit accelerated under all policies
272	on the life of the insured. For chronic illness, the death benefit you can accelerate is subject to a monthly limit. The death
273	benefit will be reduced by the amount of the death benefit you decide to accelerate. A discount factor will be applied to
274	the death benefit accelerated because it is being paid prior to the actual death benefit. As a result, the actual benefit
275	paid will be less than the amount of death benefit accelerated.
276	
277	                        The use of one benefit may reduce or eliminate other policy and rider benefits.
278	
279	The sample benefits shown assume current accelerated benefits mortality tables and interest at 6.5%. The benefits and values
280	shown above are not guaranteed. The assumptions on which they are based are subject to change by the insurer based on

309	Plan Description
310	
311	Term 30-G Form Series ICC18-20522, is a term life insurance policy that is annually renewable to age 95. Premiums are
312	level for the first 30 years and increase annually thereafter to attained age 95. This policy has no cash value and no
313	dividends are payable.
314	
315	This policy is convertible during the first 20 years from the date of issue or until age 70 if earlier, without evidence of
316	insurability to any single life permanent plan of life insurance then sold by us.
317

513	provisions of the law, the terms of the contract, and your personal situation at the time payments are made. These factors

The optional Accelerated Benefit Riders (ABR) offer you flexibility to access your death benefit if you
have a qualifying Terminal Illness, Chronic Illness, Critical Illness, Critical Injury or Alzheimer's Disease1

    If the full, available death benefit is accelerated on Dave Miller (Base), the discounted benefit is projected to be:

Terminal Illness           Chronic Illness (Monthly Benefits)  Alzheimer's Disease

Age 44 $515,814            Age 41 $4,082                                                 Age 41 $338,607
                           Age 43 $4,426                                                 Age 43 $343,715
                           Age 45 $4,775                                                 Age 45 $350,753
                           Age 49 $5,508                                                 Age 49 $371,786

                          Critical Illness OR Critical Injury - based on impact on life expectancy

                                                  At age 41, between $0 and $474,747.
                                                  At age 43, between $0 and $474,731.
                                                  At age 45, between $0 and $474,703.
                                                  At age 49, between $0 and $474,549.

                                                        What does this mean for me and my family?

                          If I become ill, there is the ability to access a portion of the death benefit NOW. This will help
                                 me and my family maintain our lifestyle...even during a challenging financial time.

                                 Depending on the severity of illness or injury, the ABR benefit, if available, will be
                           determined at time of claim. Based on impact on life expectancy, the benefit amount could

                                                                                   be $0.

The critical illness or injury amount, if any, will be determined using four different levels depending on the severity of the
illness or injury. Many illnesses actually have a minimal impact on future mortality which could mean a small or even no
benefit even with a qualifying event. At time of claim, we will assess mortality and other factors to determine the benefit
amount that may be payable. The owner has the option to decline to make a claim after receiving a benefit estimate.

You can accelerate up to 100% of the death benefit, subject to an ABR Benefit limit of $1,500,000 for terminal illness, an
ABR Benefit limit of $1,500,000 for chronic illness, an ABR Benefit limit of $1,000,000 for critical illness and critical injury
and an ABR Benefit limit of $1,500,000 for alzheimer's disease on the total death benefit accelerated under all policies
on the life of the insured. For chronic illness, the death benefit you can accelerate is subject to a monthly limit. The death
benefit will be reduced by the amount of the death benefit you decide to accelerate. A discount factor will be applied to
the death benefit accelerated because it is being paid prior to the actual death benefit. As a result, the actual benefit
paid will be less than the amount of death benefit accelerated.

                        The use of one benefit may reduce or eliminate other policy and rider benefits.

The sample benefits shown assume current accelerated benefits mortality tables and interest at 6.5%. The benefits and values
shown above are not guaranteed. The assumptions on which they are based are subject to change by the insurer based on
expectations of future anticipated or emerging experience, but are subject to any minimums and maximums stated in the
policy or rider. Future anticipated or emerging experience can include, investment earnings, mortality, the number of levels of
mortality, persistency, required reserves, required capital, hedging costs, reinsurance costs, taxes and expenses which may
affect these assumptions. Actual results may be more or less favorable. This presentation is not valid unless accompanied by
a complete Statement of Policy Cost and Benefit Information.

1 Accelerated Benefit Riders are optional, available with no additional premium, may be subject to underwriting, exclusions
and/or limitations and may not be available in all states. See the Rider Description pages for further details.

Plan Description

Term 30-G Form Series ICC18-20522, is a term life insurance policy that is annually renewable to age 95. Premiums are
level for the first 30 years and increase annually thereafter to attained age 95. This policy has no cash value and no
dividends are payable.

This policy is convertible during the first 20 years from the date of issue or until age 70 if earlier, without evidence of
insurability to any single life permanent plan of life insurance then sold by us.

Premium Payment Options

This statement assumes premiums are paid on a monthly electronic funds transfer (EFT) basis and are received at the
beginning of each billing period.

Your yearly cost will be higher if you choose to pay premiums more frequently than annually. For example, the additional
amount you will pay in the first year is as follows:

Definition of Key Terms and Column Headings

Age - The insured's age as of nearest birthday.

Face Amount � The amount used to determine the death benefit.

Guaranteed Contract Premium � The annualized guaranteed maximum premium for the term policy based on the
premium mode selected.

Guaranteed Death Benefit � The policy's guaranteed death benefit.

Policy Year � The number of years for which information is being illustrated.

Rate Class � The rate class used in this Statement of Policy Cost and Benefit Information (statement) is Elite Non-
Tobacco. The actual rate class will be determined when the application is underwritten and may vary from this statement.
If so, a revised statement will be delivered with the policy.

Tax Treatment: The Company will report any eligible distributions, under any accelerated benefits rider, subject to
existing IRS guidance and facts at the time of distribution. However, proper tax treatment for any accelerated benefits you
receive under this insurance contract depends on a number of factors. These factors include, among others, the
provisions of the law, the terms of the contract, and your personal situation at the time payments are made. These factors

content.thinking:
I've extracted the key details from Dave Miller's insurance policy document. The policy is a Term 30-G product from Life Insurance Company of the Southwest, covering a 39-year-old male non-tobacco user in Virginia with a $600,000 face amount and an initial monthly premium of $71.54 via electronic funds transfer.

The policy includes five accelerated benefit riders at no additional cost, and it's a 30-year level term that becomes annually renewable to age 95, with no cash value or dividends. Dave can convert this to a permanent plan within 20 years from issue or by age 70, whichever comes first, without needing to prove insurability again.

I'm now reviewing the accelerated benefit rider values across different ages and conditions—terminal illness, chronic illness, and Alzheimer's disease—which show the discounted amounts Dave could access if he qualifies for these benefits. I'm noticing the ledger actually uses the monthly EFT mode annualized at $858.48 rather than the true annual premium of $813.00. The guaranteed premiums level for 30 years, then spike dramatically in the post-30 period—jumping to $16,622.52 at age 69 and climbing steeply to $318,393.48 by age 94, with a cumulative total reaching nearly $2.9 million.

Now comparing across term lengths, the 30-year guaranteed level premium is $858.48 annually (or $813 in true annual mode), while shorter terms like 10, 15, and 20 years run $357.96, $465.72, and $548.04 respectively. But there's something off with the rate class breakdown—Elite should be the lowest cost, yet it's showing $858.48 while Preferred appears at $668.40, which doesn't align.

The Term 30-G product itself is straightforward: it's part of the guaranteed level premium series (Form ICC18-20522), stays level for 30 years then escalates annually through age 95, carries no cash value or dividends, and allows conversion within the first 20 years or to age 70 if that comes first without requiring medical underwriting.

Now I'm mapping out the Phase 1 workflow: I'll run a deep-research pass on NLG and LSW Term products to compile the actual claims and features, then layer in an adversarial audit to stress-test those claims before documenting everything. Unlike the IUL work where I audited already-shipped marketing, here I'm auditing the research findings themselves as they come together.

I'm weighing the token cost of the deep-research skill against the adversarial audit workflow—both are expensive operations, so I need to be strategic about scope and ordering.

Since the user explicitly wants both approaches, I'll proceed with deep-research first to build the knowledge base, then feed those findings into the workflow for claim-by-claim verification. For the research query, I should include the full product lineup—Term 10-G, 15-G, 20-G, 30-G, and any legacy Term products—to ensure comprehensive coverage.

Now I'll invoke the deep-research skill to gather detailed information on issue ages, term lengths, conversion privileges, the ABR living-benefit suite, premium structures, state variations, and foreign-national underwriting rules. Before running it, I should save the extracted PDF facts to a scratchpad so I have them available across both phases without losing context.

content.text:
I have the complete sample illustration. Let me capture the key structured facts to scratchpad (I'll need them in Phase 1 cross-check and Phase 2), then launch the research.

Key facts from `Dave Miller.pdf`:
- **Term 30-G**, LSW (Life Insurance Company of the Southwest), Form Series **ICC18-20522**
- Dave Miller, **Male 39 Elite Non-Tobacco, Virginia**; Face **$600,000**; **$71.54/mo EFT** (= $858.48/yr)
- **Annually renewable to age 95; level premium first 30 yrs, then increases annually to 95; no cash value, no dividends**
- **Convertible first 20 yrs or to age 70 if earlier, no evidence of insurability, equivalent rate class**
- 5 ABRs, no additional premium; limits $1.5M terminal / $1.5M chronic / $1.0M critical illness+injury (shared) / $1.5M Alzheimer's, "never less than $500,000", vary by state
- ABR sample values discounted & condition-specific (Terminal ~$515,814 ≈86%; Alzheimer's ~$371,786 ≈62%; Critical Ill/Injury $0–$474,549 ≈79%; Chronic monthly $4,082–$5,508)
- Series: Term 10-G / 15-G / 20-G / 30-G

# Dave Miller.pdf — extracted facts (Term 30-G / LSW sample)

Reference illustration for Arlindo Term product. Statement ID 74434, prepared 2026-07-11,
Version 26.1 A, form TC8552763(1125)3-LSW-30G. Agent: Adriano Salvador (Provo, UT).

## Policy header
- Product: **Term 30-G** — Term Life Insurance
- Carrier: **Life Insurance Company of the Southwest (LSW)**, Addison, TX
- Form Series: **ICC18-20522**
- Insured: Dave Miller, **Male, age 39, Elite Non-Tobacco**
- State: **Virginia**
- Face Amount / Death Benefit: **$600,000** (level, guaranteed)
- Initial Premium: **$71.54 Monthly (EFT)**  → annualized $858.48
- Riders: ABR (5 accelerated benefit riders, no additional premium)

## Plan description (verbatim key facts)
- "Term 30-G Form Series ICC18-20522, is a term life insurance policy that is **annually
  renewable to age 95**. Premiums are **level for the first 30 years and increase annually
  thereafter to attained age 95**. This policy has **no cash value and no dividends** are payable."
- Convertible: "**convertible during the first 20 years** from the date of issue or **until age 70
  if earlier**, without evidence of insurability to any single life permanent plan of life
  insurance then sold by us." (equivalent rate class regardless of health changes)

## Premium modes — year 1 (Term 30-G)
| Mode | Each payment | Total/yr |
|---|---|---|
| Annual | $813.00 | $813.00 |
| Semi-Annual | $414.63 ×2 | $829.26 |
| Quarterly | $211.38 ×4 | $845.52 |
| Monthly (EFT) | $71.54 ×12 | $858.48 |

Ledger uses the Monthly-EFT annualized figure **$858.48/yr**, level for 30 years
(yr30 cumulative $25,754.40 = 858.48×30). After year 30 (age 69+) ARt premiums escalate
annually to age 95 (e.g., ~$16,622 → ~$318,393 at age 94).

## Ledger columns (ONLY these — no cash value)
Policy Year · Age (nearest birthday) · Guaranteed Contract Premium · Guaranteed Death Benefit
DB constant $600,000 all years. Age at yr1 = 39.

## Level Period Comparison (term-vs-term, annualized monthly-EFT)
Term 10-G $357.96/yr · Term 15-G $465.72/yr · Term 20-G $548.04/yr · Term 30-G $858.48/yr
(annual-mode: $339 / $441 / $519 / $813). "G" = guaranteed level-premium series.

## ABR (Accelerated Benefit Riders) — 5, no additional premium
Terminal Illness · Chronic Illness · Critical Illness · Critical Injury · Alzheimer's Disease
- Discounted, condition-specific (NOT a flat %). Sample "if fully accelerated" values:
  - Terminal: age 44 $515,814 (page 2 headline age 49 $515,807) ≈ 86% of $600k
  - Chronic (MONTHLY benefit): age 41 $4,082 / 43 $4,426 / 45 $4,775 / 49 $5,508
  - Alzheimer's: age 41 $338,607 / 43 $343,715 / 45 $350,753 / 49 $371,786 ≈ 62%
  - Critical Illness OR Injury: severity-based, age 41 $0–$474,747 … age 49 $0–$474,549 ≈ 79%
- Trigger notes: Terminal = death expected in **24 months or less**; Chronic = unable to perform
  **2 of 6 ADLs** (bathing, continence, dressing, eating, toileting, transferring) or cognitive
  impairment; Critical Illness list (cancer, heart attack, stroke, ALS, organ transplant, etc.);
  Critical Injury (coma, paralysis, severe burns, TBI); Alzheimer's = MMSE < 20/30 + dementia dx.
- Form series: Terminal/Critical ICC22-20805; Critical Injury ICC22-20806; Alzheimer's ICC22-20818;
  Chronic 8095VA (VA state form).
- Limits (per insured, all policies): **$1.5M terminal, $1.5M chronic, $1.0M critical illness +
  critical injury (shared), $1.5M Alzheimer's**; "never be less than $500,000"; vary by state.
  Chronic subject to a monthly maximum. Sample assumes current ABR mortality tables + 6.5% interest.

## Compliance/disclaimer language present
- "Guarantees are dependent upon the claims-paying ability of the issuing company."
- "No bank/credit union guarantee | Not a deposit | Not FDIC/NCUA insured | May lose value."
- Income-tax-free death benefit IRC §101(a)(1). ABR tax treatment complex (IRC §101(g)).
- "Riders are optional and may require additional premium." / "may not be available in all states."

  - Critical Illness OR Injury: severity-based, age 41 $0–$474,747 … age 49 $0–$474,549 ≈ 79%
- Trigger notes: Terminal = death expected in **24 months or less**; Chronic = unable to perform
  **2 of 6 ADLs** (bathing, continence, dressing, eating, toileting, transferring) or cognitive
  impairment; Critical Illness list (cancer, heart attack, stroke, ALS, organ transplant, etc.);
  Critical Injury (coma, paralysis, severe burns, TBI); Alzheimer's = MMSE < 20/30 + dementia dx.
- Form series: Terminal/Critical ICC22-20805; Critical Injury ICC22-20806; Alzheimer's ICC22-20818;
  Chronic 8095VA (VA state form).
- Limits (per insured, all policies): **$1.5M terminal, $1.5M chronic, $1.0M critical illness +
  critical injury (shared), $1.5M Alzheimer's**; "never be less than $500,000"; vary by state.
  Chronic subject to a monthly maximum. Sample assumes current ABR mortality tables + 6.5% interest.

## Compliance/disclaimer language present
- "Guarantees are dependent upon the claims-paying ability of the issuing company."
- "No bank/credit union guarantee | Not a deposit | Not FDIC/NCUA insured | May lose value."
- Income-tax-free death benefit IRC §101(a)(1). ABR tax treatment complex (IRC §101(g)).
- "Riders are optional and may require additional premium." / "may not be available in all states."

Prioritize NLG/LSW primary sources: nationallife.com, the Life Product Quick Reference Chart, agent guides, product guides, rider brochures, and the Foreign National Guidelines. Distinguish guaranteed/structural facts from rate-sheet-driven current rates (which change often). Produce a cited report with source URLs.

content.text:
Run the "deep-research" workflow.

Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report.

When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.

Phases:
- Scope: Decompose question (from args) into 5 search angles
- Search: 5 parallel WebSearch agents, one per angle
- Fetch: URL-dedup, fetch top 15 sources, extract falsifiable claims
- Verify: 3-vote adversarial verification per claim (need 2/3 refutes to kill)
- Synthesize: Merge semantic dupes, rank by confidence, cite sources

import type { Rider } from './presentation'
import { DEFAULT_IUL_RIDERS } from './riders'

/**
 * National Life Group IUL product registry. FlexLife is live now; the other four
 * are defined and ready to enable (`available: true`) — adding a product is a
 * config addition here, not a code change elsewhere.
 *
 * Only GUARANTEED, verified facts are encoded (min face, guaranteed min rate,
 * carrier, positioning). Current caps / participation / bonus rates are
 * rate-sheet-driven and deliberately NOT stored.
 */
export type ProductFocus = 'accumulation' | 'protection' | 'survivorship'

export interface ProductDefinition {
  id: string
  name: string
  /** Issuing carrier(s): National Life (NL), LSW, or both. */
  carrier: 'NL' | 'LSW' | 'NL / LSW'
  focus: ProductFocus
  minFace: number
  guaranteedMinRatePct: number
  /** pt-BR one-liner shown when picking a product. */
  positioning: string
  /** Whether the app currently supports building with this product. */
  available: boolean
}

export const NLG_PRODUCTS: ProductDefinition[] = [
  {
    id: 'flexlife',
    name: 'FlexLife',
    carrier: 'NL / LSW',
    focus: 'accumulation',
    minFace: 50_000,
    guaranteedMinRatePct: 1.75,
    positioning:
      'IUL de acumulação e proteção flexível — crescimento do valor acumulado + renda futura (LIBR).',
    available: true,
  },
  {
    id: 'peaklife',
    name: 'PeakLife',
    carrier: 'NL',
    focus: 'accumulation',
    minFace: 1_000_000,
    guaranteedMinRatePct: 1,
    positioning: 'IUL para alto patrimônio (face ≥ US$ 1 mi) — acumulação e financiamento de prêmio.',
    available: false,
  },
  {
    id: 'summitlife',
    name: 'SummitLife',
    carrier: 'LSW',
    focus: 'accumulation',
    minFace: 1_000_000,
    guaranteedMinRatePct: 1,
    positioning: 'IUL para alto patrimônio (face ≥ US$ 1 mi) — preservar e amplificar patrimônio.',
    available: false,
  },
  {
    id: 'rapidprotect',
    name: 'RapidProtect',
    carrier: 'NL / LSW',
    focus: 'protection',
    minFace: 50_000,
    guaranteedMinRatePct: 2,
    positioning: 'IUL de proteção com emissão simplificada (sem exame) — foco no benefício por morte.',
    available: false,
  },
  {
    id: 'survivorlife',
    name: 'SurvivorLife',
    carrier: 'NL / LSW',
    focus: 'survivorship',
    minFace: 250_000,
    guaranteedMinRatePct: 1,
    positioning: 'IUL de segunda morte (dois segurados) — planejamento sucessório e transferência.',
    available: false,
  },
]

const DEFAULT_PRODUCT_ID = 'flexlife'

export function getProduct(id: string | undefined): ProductDefinition {
  return NLG_PRODUCTS.find((p) => p.id === id) ?? NLG_PRODUCTS[0]
}

export function availableProducts(): ProductDefinition[] {
  return NLG_PRODUCTS.filter((p) => p.available)
}

/** Default rider suite for a product. All NLG IULs share the ABR suite today. */
export function defaultRidersForProduct(_id: string = DEFAULT_PRODUCT_ID): Rider[] {
  return DEFAULT_IUL_RIDERS.map((r) => ({ ...r }))
}

1	import { z } from 'zod'
2	
3	/**
4	 * The single source of truth for a presentation's INPUT data.
5	 * The zod schema validates data-entry, persistence, and JSON import; the TS
6	 * types are inferred from it. The presentation renderer never reads this shape
7	 * directly — it reads a `DerivedPresentation` produced by a `CalculationEngine`.
8	 *
9	 * Fields are intentionally lenient (optional / defaulted) so drafts autosave
10	 * mid-edit; `isPresentable()` gates whether there's enough to present.
11	 */
12	
13	export const CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION = 1
14	
15	export const currencyCodeSchema = z.enum(['USD', 'BRL'])
16	export type CurrencyCode = z.infer<typeof currencyCodeSchema>
17	
18	export const productTypeSchema = z.enum(['iul', 'annuity'])
19	export type ProductType = z.infer<typeof productTypeSchema>
20	
21	/** Agent / company / carrier branding. Lives in Foundation; slides render it. */
22	export const brandingSchema = z.object({
23	  agentName: z.string().default(''),
24	  agentTitle: z.string().default('Agente Financeiro Licenciado'),
25	  agentLicense: z.string().default(''),
26	  company: z.string().default('Second Chance Financial'),
27	  carrier: z.string().default('National Life Group'),
28	})
29	export type Branding = z.infer<typeof brandingSchema>
30	
31	export const clientSchema = z.object({
32	  name: z.string().default(''),
33	  age: z.number().int().min(0).max(120).optional(),
34	  sex: z.enum(['M', 'F']).optional(),
35	  notes: z.string().optional(),
36	})
37	export type Client = z.infer<typeof clientSchema>
38	
39	/**
40	 * A living-benefit rider. Modifiable per the user's request: each can be
41	 * toggled, its accessible living-benefit percentage set (standard riders access
42	 * up to 80%; the Premium Chronic Care rider up to 100%), and flagged as
43	 * additional-cost.
44	 */
45	export const riderSchema = z.object({
46	  id: z.string(),
47	  label: z.string(),
48	  englishLabel: z.string().optional(),
49	  included: z.boolean().default(true),
50	  percent: z.number().min(0).max(100).default(80),
51	  additionalCost: z.boolean().default(false),
52	  category: z.enum(['included', 'iul_exclusive', 'optional']).default('included'),
53	  /** Lifetime maximum accelerated amount (USD), when the rider has a dollar cap. */
54	  lifetimeMax: z.number().optional(),
55	  note: z.string().optional(),
56	})
57	export type Rider = z.infer<typeof riderSchema>
58	
59	/** One row copied off the carrier illustration (all amounts in displayCurrency). */
60	export const yearlyRowSchema = z.object({
61	  policyYear: z.number().int(),
62	  age: z.number().int().optional(),
63	  premiumPaid: z.number().optional(),
64	  accumulatedValue: z.number().optional(),
65	  cashSurrenderValue: z.number().optional(),
66	  deathBenefit: z.number().optional(),
67	})
68	export type YearlyRow = z.infer<typeof yearlyRowSchema>
69	
70	/** IUL-specific fields (present when productType === 'iul'). */
71	export const iulSchema = z.object({
72	  premium: z.number().nonnegative().optional(),
73	  premiumMode: z.enum(['monthly', 'annual']).default('monthly'),
74	  deathBenefit: z.number().nonnegative().optional(),
75	  livingBenefit: z.number().nonnegative().optional(),
76	  livingBenefitPercent: z.number().min(0).max(100).default(80),
77	  assumedRatePct: z.number().optional(),
78	  /** How many years the client pays the deposit (shown on the coverage slide). */
79	  paymentYears: z.number().int().positive().optional(),
80	  projectionYears: z.number().int().positive().optional(),
81	  projectedAccumulatedValue: z.number().optional(),
82	  /** Cash surrender value at the projection year — what the client actually gets on withdrawal. */
83	  projectedCashSurrenderValue: z.number().optional(),
84	  incomeOptionAnnual: z.number().optional(),
85	  incomeToAge: z.number().int().optional(),
86	  riders: z.array(riderSchema).default([]),
87	  /**
88	   * 'typed' = use the numbers the agent typed off the carrier illustration
89	   * (PassthroughEngine). 'estimate' = let the app compute an approximate
90	   * projection (IulProjectionEngine). Default 'typed' (the official illustration
91	   * is authoritative).
92	   */
93	  projectionSource: z.enum(['typed', 'estimate']).default('typed'),
94	})
95	export type IulInputs = z.infer<typeof iulSchema>
96	
97	/** Annuity fields — reserved for a later wave; not built in the first release. */
98	export const annuitySchema = z
99	  .object({
100	    initialInvestment: z.number().optional(),
101	    bonusPercent: z.number().optional(),
102	    glirAnnual: z.number().optional(),
103	  })
104	  .partial()
105	export type AnnuityInputs = z.infer<typeof annuitySchema>
106	
107	export const presentationInputsSchema = z.object({
108	  schemaVersion: z.number().int().default(CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION),
109	  id: z.string(),
110	  createdAt: z.string(),
111	  updatedAt: z.string(),
112	  productType: productTypeSchema.default('iul'),
113	  /** NLG product id from the product registry (@domain/model/products), e.g. 'flexlife'. */
114	  productId: z.string().default('flexlife'),
115	  title: z.string().default(''),
116	  /** Language the client-facing presentation (slides/PDF/PPTX) is generated in. */
117	  presentationLanguage: z.enum(['pt', 'en', 'es']).default('pt'),
118	  displayCurrency: currencyCodeSchema.default('USD'),
119	  branding: brandingSchema.default({}),
120	  client: clientSchema.default({}),
121	  iul: iulSchema.default({}),
122	  annuity: annuitySchema.optional(),
123	  yearlyRows: z.array(yearlyRowSchema).default([]),
124	  highlightYears: z.array(z.number().int()).default([]),
125	  disclaimers: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
126	})
127	export type PresentationInputs = z.infer<typeof presentationInputsSchema>
128	
129	/** Enough data to actually present? (keeps the "Present" button honest) */
130	export function isPresentable(p: PresentationInputs): boolean {
131	  const hasClient = p.client.name.trim().length > 0
132	  if (p.productType === 'iul') {
133	    const iul = p.iul
134	    const hasNumbers =
135	      iul.deathBenefit != null ||
136	      iul.projectedAccumulatedValue != null ||
137	      p.yearlyRows.length > 0
138	    return hasClient && hasNumbers
139	  }
140	  return hasClient
141	}
142

import { z } from 'zod'

/**
 * The single source of truth for a presentation's INPUT data.
 * The zod schema validates data-entry, persistence, and JSON import; the TS
 * types are inferred from it. The presentation renderer never reads this shape
 * directly — it reads a `DerivedPresentation` produced by a `CalculationEngine`.
 *
 * Fields are intentionally lenient (optional / defaulted) so drafts autosave
 * mid-edit; `isPresentable()` gates whether there's enough to present.
 */

export const CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION = 1

export const currencyCodeSchema = z.enum(['USD', 'BRL'])
export type CurrencyCode = z.infer<typeof currencyCodeSchema>

export const productTypeSchema = z.enum(['iul', 'annuity'])
export type ProductType = z.infer<typeof productTypeSchema>

/** Agent / company / carrier branding. Lives in Foundation; slides render it. */
export const brandingSchema = z.object({
  agentName: z.string().default(''),
  agentTitle: z.string().default('Agente Financeiro Licenciado'),
  agentLicense: z.string().default(''),
  company: z.string().default('Second Chance Financial'),
  carrier: z.string().default('National Life Group'),
})
export type Branding = z.infer<typeof brandingSchema>

export const clientSchema = z.object({
  name: z.string().default(''),
  age: z.number().int().min(0).max(120).optional(),
  sex: z.enum(['M', 'F']).optional(),
  notes: z.string().optional(),
})
export type Client = z.infer<typeof clientSchema>

/**
 * A living-benefit rider. Modifiable per the user's request: each can be
 * toggled, its accessible living-benefit percentage set (standard riders access
 * up to 80%; the Premium Chronic Care rider up to 100%), and flagged as
 * additional-cost.
 */
export const riderSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string(),
  label: z.string(),
  englishLabel: z.string().optional(),
  included: z.boolean().default(true),
  percent: z.number().min(0).max(100).default(80),
  additionalCost: z.boolean().default(false),
  category: z.enum(['included', 'iul_exclusive', 'optional']).default('included'),
  /** Lifetime maximum accelerated amount (USD), when the rider has a dollar cap. */
  lifetimeMax: z.number().optional(),
  note: z.string().optional(),
})
export type Rider = z.infer<typeof riderSchema>

/** One row copied off the carrier illustration (all amounts in displayCurrency). */
export const yearlyRowSchema = z.object({
  policyYear: z.number().int(),
  age: z.number().int().optional(),
  premiumPaid: z.number().optional(),
  accumulatedValue: z.number().optional(),
  cashSurrenderValue: z.number().optional(),
  deathBenefit: z.number().optional(),
})
export type YearlyRow = z.infer<typeof yearlyRowSchema>

/** IUL-specific fields (present when productType === 'iul'). */
export const iulSchema = z.object({
  premium: z.number().nonnegative().optional(),
  premiumMode: z.enum(['monthly', 'annual']).default('monthly'),
  deathBenefit: z.number().nonnegative().optional(),
  livingBenefit: z.number().nonnegative().optional(),
  livingBenefitPercent: z.number().min(0).max(100).default(80),
  assumedRatePct: z.number().optional(),
  /** How many years the client pays the deposit (shown on the coverage slide). */
  paymentYears: z.number().int().positive().optional(),
  projectionYears: z.number().int().positive().optional(),
  projectedAccumulatedValue: z.number().optional(),
  /** Cash surrender value at the projection year — what the client actually gets on withdrawal. */
  projectedCashSurrenderValue: z.number().optional(),
  incomeOptionAnnual: z.number().optional(),
  incomeToAge: z.number().int().optional(),
  riders: z.array(riderSchema).default([]),
  /**
   * 'typed' = use the numbers the agent typed off the carrier illustration
   * (PassthroughEngine). 'estimate' = let the app compute an approximate
   * projection (IulProjectionEngine). Default 'typed' (the official illustration
   * is authoritative).
   */
  projectionSource: z.enum(['typed', 'estimate']).default('typed'),
})
export type IulInputs = z.infer<typeof iulSchema>

/** Annuity fields — reserved for a later wave; not built in the first release. */
export const annuitySchema = z
  .object({
    initialInvestment: z.number().optional(),
    bonusPercent: z.number().optional(),
    glirAnnual: z.number().optional(),
  })
  .partial()
export type AnnuityInputs = z.infer<typeof annuitySchema>

export const presentationInputsSchema = z.object({
  schemaVersion: z.number().int().default(CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION),
  id: z.string(),
  createdAt: z.string(),
  updatedAt: z.string(),
  productType: productTypeSchema.default('iul'),
  /** NLG product id from the product registry (@domain/model/products), e.g. 'flexlife'. */
  productId: z.string().default('flexlife'),
  title: z.string().default(''),
  /** Language the client-facing presentation (slides/PDF/PPTX) is generated in. */
  presentationLanguage: z.enum(['pt', 'en', 'es']).default('pt'),
  displayCurrency: currencyCodeSchema.default('USD'),
  branding: brandingSchema.default({}),
  client: clientSchema.default({}),
  iul: iulSchema.default({}),
  annuity: annuitySchema.optional(),
  yearlyRows: z.array(yearlyRowSchema).default([]),
  highlightYears: z.array(z.number().int()).default([]),
  disclaimers: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
})
export type PresentationInputs = z.infer<typeof presentationInputsSchema>

/** Enough data to actually present? (keeps the "Present" button honest) */
export function isPresentable(p: PresentationInputs): boolean {
  const hasClient = p.client.name.trim().length > 0
  if (p.productType === 'iul') {
    const iul = p.iul
    const hasNumbers =
      iul.deathBenefit != null ||
      iul.projectedAccumulatedValue != null ||
      p.yearlyRows.length > 0
    return hasClient && hasNumbers
  }
  return hasClient
}

content.subject: Baseline setup: install, build, test
content.description: npm install, confirm npm run build passes and npm test (19 tests) pass on clean main before any work.

content.subject: Phase 1: NLG Term knowledge base + adversarial audit
content.description: deep-research NLG/LSW Term life; ultracode Workflow adversarial audit (per-claim verifier + skeptic vs primary sources + synthesis); produce docs/knowledge/national-life-term.md (confidence+verdict+URLs), docs/knowledge/term-audit-<date>.json (raw), update knowledge README. Feature branch + draft PR.

content.subject: Phase 2: Build Term product type
content.description: Using Dave Miller.pdf (Term 30-G) as reference: add productType 'term', Term fields, Term registry entry, Term-specific slide set (drop IUL accumulation/projection/withdraw; add premium-schedule table + conversion + ABR + term-vs-permanent). Localize PT/EN/ES. Verify Term deck present+PDF+.pptx matches sample. Feature branch + draft PR.

content.subject: Phase 3: UI + presentation polish (incl. mobile)
content.description: Polish UI + presentation including mobile: 390px header/nav overflow, present-mode portrait hint. Feature branch + draft PR.

lastPrompt: You're picking up Arlindo, a client-side React/TS/Tailwind app that turns insurance-illustration numbers into polished client presentations for an older agent (Second Chance Financial · National Life…

content.thinking: Now I'm mapping out Phase 2 by adding `'term'` to the product type schema, creating a term object and Term product entry, plus the associated riders. I need to dig into the key files that handle this — riders.ts, buildSlides.tsx, the IUL slide templates, presentationCopy.ts, derived.ts, PassthroughEngine, and the export logic — though that's quite a bit to cover, so I'll start by focusing on riders.ts first.

content.text: Model understood. Let me read the rider suite, the derived contract, and the slide builder — the core seams for Phase 2 — while research runs.

file.content:
import type {
  Branding,
  CurrencyCode,
  ProductType,
  Rider,
  YearlyRow,
} from './presentation'

/**
 * What the presentation renderer reads. Produced by a `CalculationEngine`.
 * Today `PassthroughEngine` reshapes typed numbers into this; later an
 * `IulProjectionEngine` will compute it from assumptions — slides never change.
 */
export interface DerivedPresentation {
  meta: {
    productType: ProductType
    clientName: string
    clientAge?: number
    productName: string
    branding: Branding
    currency: CurrencyCode
    language: 'pt' | 'en' | 'es'
    preparedOn: string
    generatedBy: 'passthrough' | 'iul-engine' | 'annuity-engine'
    engineVersion: string
  }
  headline: {
    premium?: number
    premiumMode?: 'monthly' | 'annual'
    paymentYears?: number
    totalPremiumsPaid?: number
    projectedAccumulatedValue?: number
    projectedCashSurrenderValue?: number
    deathBenefit?: number
    livingBenefit?: number
    livingBenefitPercent?: number
    incomeOptionAnnual?: number
    incomeToAge?: number
    projectionYears?: number
  }
  series: {
    policyYears: number[]
    ages?: number[]
    accumulatedValue?: number[]
    cashSurrenderValue?: number[]
    deathBenefit?: number[]
    premiumPaid?: number[]
  }
  table: YearlyRow[]
  riders: Rider[]
  disclaimers: string[]
}

content.content:
1	import type { ReactNode } from 'react'
2	import type { DerivedPresentation } from '@domain/model/derived'
3	import { CoverSlide } from './iul/CoverSlide'
4	import { HeadlineSlide } from './iul/HeadlineSlide'
5	import { ExplainerSlide } from './iul/ExplainerSlide'
6	import { CoverageRidersSlide } from './iul/CoverageRidersSlide'
7	import { ProjectionSlide } from './iul/ProjectionSlide'
8	import { TableSlide } from './iul/TableSlide'
9	import { WithdrawVsIncomeSlide } from './iul/WithdrawVsIncomeSlide'
10	import { ComparisonSlide } from './iul/ComparisonSlide'
11	import { DisclaimersSlide } from './iul/DisclaimersSlide'
12	
13	export interface BuiltSlide {
14	  id: string
15	  title: string
16	  node: ReactNode
17	}
18	
19	/**
20	 * The single ordered slide set for a presentation. Present mode (W3) and PDF
21	 * export (W4) both consume this — they compose the same slides, differing only
22	 * by render mode. Slides with no data are omitted.
23	 */
24	export function buildSlides(derived: DerivedPresentation): BuiltSlide[] {
25	  const slides: BuiltSlide[] = [
26	    { id: 'cover', title: 'Capa', node: <CoverSlide derived={derived} /> },
27	    { id: 'headline', title: 'Resumo', node: <HeadlineSlide derived={derived} /> },
28	    { id: 'explainer', title: 'O que é IUL', node: <ExplainerSlide derived={derived} /> },
29	    { id: 'coverage', title: 'Cobertura', node: <CoverageRidersSlide derived={derived} /> },
30	    { id: 'projection', title: 'Projeção', node: <ProjectionSlide derived={derived} /> },
31	  ]
32	
33	  if (derived.table.length > 0) {
34	    slides.push({ id: 'table', title: 'Ano a ano', node: <TableSlide derived={derived} /> })
35	  }
36	
37	  slides.push(
38	    {
39	      id: 'options',
40	      title: 'Duas opções',
41	      node: <WithdrawVsIncomeSlide derived={derived} />,
42	    },
43	    { id: 'comparison', title: 'Termo vs IUL', node: <ComparisonSlide derived={derived} /> },
44	    { id: 'disclaimers', title: 'Avisos', node: <DisclaimersSlide derived={derived} /> },
45	  )
46	
47	  return slides
48	}
49

import type { ReactNode } from 'react'
import type { DerivedPresentation } from '@domain/model/derived'
import { CoverSlide } from './iul/CoverSlide'
import { HeadlineSlide } from './iul/HeadlineSlide'
import { ExplainerSlide } from './iul/ExplainerSlide'
import { CoverageRidersSlide } from './iul/CoverageRidersSlide'
import { ProjectionSlide } from './iul/ProjectionSlide'
import { TableSlide } from './iul/TableSlide'
import { WithdrawVsIncomeSlide } from './iul/WithdrawVsIncomeSlide'
import { ComparisonSlide } from './iul/ComparisonSlide'
import { DisclaimersSlide } from './iul/DisclaimersSlide'

export interface BuiltSlide {
  id: string
  title: string
  node: ReactNode
}

/**
 * The single ordered slide set for a presentation. Present mode (W3) and PDF
 * export (W4) both consume this — they compose the same slides, differing only
 * by render mode. Slides with no data are omitted.
 */
export function buildSlides(derived: DerivedPresentation): BuiltSlide[] {
  const slides: BuiltSlide[] = [
    { id: 'cover', title: 'Capa', node: <CoverSlide derived={derived} /> },
    { id: 'headline', title: 'Resumo', node: <HeadlineSlide derived={derived} /> },
    { id: 'explainer', title: 'O que é IUL', node: <ExplainerSlide derived={derived} /> },
    { id: 'coverage', title: 'Cobertura', node: <CoverageRidersSlide derived={derived} /> },
    { id: 'projection', title: 'Projeção', node: <ProjectionSlide derived={derived} /> },
  ]

  if (derived.table.length > 0) {
    slides.push({ id: 'table', title: 'Ano a ano', node: <TableSlide derived={derived} /> })
  }

  slides.push(
    {
      id: 'options',
      title: 'Duas opções',
      node: <WithdrawVsIncomeSlide derived={derived} />,
    },
    { id: 'comparison', title: 'Termo vs IUL', node: <ComparisonSlide derived={derived} /> },
    { id: 'disclaimers', title: 'Avisos', node: <DisclaimersSlide derived={derived} /> },
  )

  return slides
}

I'm seeing a discrepancy in how the riders are documented — the FlexLife riders reference a shared pool structure, but the Dave Miller term illustration breaks down the benefits as per-condition limits across terminal illness, chronic illness, and critical illness categories.

The key tension is in the phrasing: "under all accelerated benefit riders applying to the same insured" could mean either one combined pool or separate per-condition pools. The FlexLife audit determined they share a single $1.5M Pool A, yet the term illustration lists them as distinct $1.5M amounts, which creates exactly the ambiguity the audit flagged. Critical Illness and Critical Injury are explicitly grouped together under one $1M cap, but the other conditions aren't as clearly consolidated.

This is a strong claim for the adversarial audit to resolve. 1	import type { PresentationInputs, YearlyRow } from '@domain/model/presentation'
2	import type { DerivedPresentation } from '@domain/model/derived'
3	import {
4	  defaultProductNameFor,
5	  disclaimersFor,
6	  translateRider,
7	} from '@domain/presentationCopy'
8	import type { CalculationEngine } from './CalculationEngine'
9	
10	/** Reshapes the salesman's typed illustration numbers into a DerivedPresentation. No math. */
11	export class PassthroughEngine implements CalculationEngine {
12	  readonly id = 'passthrough' as const
13	  readonly version = '1.0.0'
14	
15	  compute(inputs: PresentationInputs): DerivedPresentation {
16	    const rows = [...inputs.yearlyRows].sort((a, b) => a.policyYear - b.policyYear)
17	    const iul = inputs.iul
18	    const lang = inputs.presentationLanguage
19	
20	    const totalPremiumsPaid =
21	      rows.length > 0
22	        ? sum(rows.map((r) => r.premiumPaid ?? 0))
23	        : premiumTotal(inputs)
24	
25	    // Living benefit is death benefit × accessible %, unless an explicit dollar
26	    // figure was entered (kept for the rare illustration that prints a different one).
27	    const resolvedDeathBenefit = iul.deathBenefit ?? lastValue(rows, 'deathBenefit')
28	    const livingBenefit =
29	      iul.livingBenefit ??
30	      (resolvedDeathBenefit != null && iul.livingBenefitPercent
31	        ? Math.round((resolvedDeathBenefit * iul.livingBenefitPercent) / 100)
32	        : undefined)
33	
34	    return {
35	      meta: {
36	        productType: inputs.productType,
37	        clientName: inputs.client.name,
38	        clientAge: inputs.client.age,
39	        productName: inputs.title || defaultProductName(inputs, lang),
40	        branding: inputs.branding,
41	        currency: inputs.displayCurrency,
42	        language: lang,
43	        preparedOn: inputs.updatedAt,
44	        generatedBy: 'passthrough',
45	        engineVersion: this.version,
46	      },
47	      headline: {
48	        premium: iul.premium,
49	        premiumMode: iul.premiumMode,
50	        paymentYears: iul.paymentYears,
51	        totalPremiumsPaid: totalPremiumsPaid || undefined,
52	        projectedAccumulatedValue:
53	          iul.projectedAccumulatedValue ?? lastValue(rows, 'accumulatedValue'),
54	        projectedCashSurrenderValue:
55	          iul.projectedCashSurrenderValue ?? lastValue(rows, 'cashSurrenderValue'),
56	        deathBenefit: resolvedDeathBenefit,
57	        livingBenefit,
58	        livingBenefitPercent: iul.livingBenefitPercent,
59	        incomeOptionAnnual: iul.incomeOptionAnnual,
60	        incomeToAge: iul.incomeToAge,
61	        projectionYears: iul.projectionYears ?? (rows.length || undefined),
62	      },
63	      series: {
64	        policyYears: rows.map((r) => r.policyYear),
65	        ages: pick(rows, 'age'),
66	        accumulatedValue: pick(rows, 'accumulatedValue'),
67	        cashSurrenderValue: pick(rows, 'cashSurrenderValue'),
68	        deathBenefit: pick(rows, 'deathBenefit'),
69	        premiumPaid: pick(rows, 'premiumPaid'),
70	      },
71	      table: rows,
72	      riders: iul.riders.filter((r) => r.included).map((r) => translateRider(r, lang)),
73	      disclaimers: disclaimersFor(lang, inputs.disclaimers),
74	    }
75	  }
76	}
77	
78	function defaultProductName(
79	  inputs: PresentationInputs,
80	  lang: PresentationInputs['presentationLanguage'],
81	): string {
82	  if (inputs.productType !== 'iul') {
83	    return lang === 'en'
84	      ? 'Retirement Plan'
85	      : lang === 'es'
86	        ? 'Plan de Jubilación'
87	        : 'Plano de Aposentadoria'
88	  }
89	  return defaultProductNameFor(lang)
90	}
91	
92	function premiumTotal(inputs: PresentationInputs): number {
93	  const { premium, premiumMode, projectionYears } = inputs.iul
94	  if (premium == null || projectionYears == null) return 0
95	  const perYear = premiumMode === 'monthly' ? premium * 12 : premium
96	  return perYear * projectionYears
97	}
98	
99	function sum(xs: number[]): number {
100	  return xs.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0)
101	}
102	
103	/** Returns the column as an array only if at least one row has a value, else undefined. */
104	function pick(rows: YearlyRow[], key: keyof YearlyRow): number[] | undefined {
105	  if (!rows.some((r) => r[key] != null)) return undefined
106	  return rows.map((r) => (r[key] ?? 0) as number)
107	}
108	
109	function lastValue(rows: YearlyRow[], key: keyof YearlyRow): number | undefined {
110	  for (let i = rows.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
111	    const v = rows[i][key]
112	    if (v != null) return v as number
113	  }
114	  return undefined
115	}

1	import type { PresentationInputs } from '@domain/model/presentation'
2	import type { DerivedPresentation } from '@domain/model/derived'
3	import type { CalculationEngine } from './CalculationEngine'
4	import { PassthroughEngine } from './PassthroughEngine'
5	import { IulProjectionEngine } from './IulProjectionEngine'
6	
7	export type { CalculationEngine } from './CalculationEngine'
8	export { PassthroughEngine } from './PassthroughEngine'
9	export { IulProjectionEngine } from './IulProjectionEngine'
10	
11	const passthrough = new PassthroughEngine()
12	const iulEngine = new IulProjectionEngine()
13	
14	/**
15	 * Selects the engine for a presentation. Default is passthrough (the agent typed
16	 * the official illustration numbers). When the agent opts into the app-computed
17	 * estimate (`iul.projectionSource === 'estimate'`), the approximate
18	 * `IulProjectionEngine` runs instead — call sites (derive) are unchanged.
19	 */
20	export function selectEngine(inputs: PresentationInputs): CalculationEngine {
21	  if (inputs.productType === 'iul' && inputs.iul.projectionSource === 'estimate') {
22	    return iulEngine
23	  }
24	  return passthrough
25	}
26	
27	export function derive(inputs: PresentationInputs): DerivedPresentation {
28	  return selectEngine(inputs).compute(inputs)
29	}

content.thinking:
The user wants to understand why the first two PDF-reading methods didn't work. I actually did manage to read the PDF successfully using `pdftotext`, so I have the full content. The Read tool failed because `pdftoppm` from the poppler library isn't available on the system—while `pdftotext` is installed, the image rendering binary isn't.

The PDF MCP viewer tool failed for a different reason: it runs in a sandboxed environment with no access to the local Windows filesystem, so it can't reach files on `C:\Users\...`. I've already extracted all 16 pages of text content, so I have what I need. The only thing text extraction can't provide is the visual design—colors, logos, chart styling—which would help me match the presentation's appearance. If they want me to see the visual layout, installing the full poppler package with `pdftoppm` would help.

content.text:
Good news first: **I can see it.** The third method — `pdftotext -layout` (poppler) — worked and pulled all 16 pages of text, so I already have the complete Dave Miller illustration: the header, premium modes, the full ledger (Year·Age·Premium·DB), all five ABR sample values, the conversion language, and every disclaimer. I've saved those facts and I'm not blocked.

Here's why the first two failed:

1. **The Read tool** renders PDF pages to *images* using poppler's `pdftoppm` binary — and on this machine only `pdftotext` is on PATH, not `pdftoppm` (the Git-for-Windows mingw64 bundle shipped one but not the other). So Read couldn't rasterize the pages.
2. **The PDF viewer MCP tool** (`display_pdf`) runs in a cloud sandbox with an empty allowed-directories list — it literally has no path into your local `C:\` filesystem, so it can't open a local file at all (only HTTPS URLs or its own registered roots).

`pdftotext` sidestepped both: it reads the local file directly and extracts text, no rendering and no sandbox.

**Do you need to do anything? No — for this task, text is everything I need.** The numbers, tables, rider descriptions, and legal disclaimers all came through cleanly, and Arlindo generates its *own* deck in the SCF navy-and-gold design system rather than mimicking the carrier illustration's visual layout, so I don't need to *see* the PDF's styling.

**If you'd like me to see it visually anyway** (e.g., to sanity-check a garbled table or match a specific visual detail), the clean fix is to install full poppler so `pdftoppm` exists:
- `choco install poppler` (or `scoop install poppler`), then restart the shell so it's on PATH — after that the Read tool can render any PDF page as an image.
- Or simplest: just screenshot the page(s) you care about and paste them here.

Either is optional. Meanwhile the deep-research workflow is still running in the background; I'll continue Phase 1 the moment it reports back.

content.text:
That's the one — **`oschwartz10612.Poppler`** (the standard poppler-windows build, v25.07). It includes `pdftoppm.exe`. Here's the walkthrough.

## The catch to know up front
Two things won't be true immediately after install:
1. Poppler's tools aren't single `.exe` files — `pdftoppm` needs its sibling DLLs, so it has to be run from (or PATH-pointed at) poppler's `Library\bin` folder.
2. **My running session captured its environment at launch.** Even once `pdftoppm` is on your PATH, my Bash/Read tools won't see it until **Claude Code is fully restarted**. So the last step is a restart.

Because winget may pop a UAC prompt and prints interactive output my non-interactive shell can't handle, **run these in your own PowerShell window**, not through me.

## Steps

**1. Install it**
```powershell
winget install -e --id oschwartz10612.Poppler
```

**2. Open a brand-new PowerShell window** (so it picks up any PATH winget set) and test:
```powershell
pdftoppm -h
```
- If you see poppler's help/usage text → **skip to step 4.** ✅
- If you get *"pdftoppm is not recognized"* → do step 3.

**3. (Only if needed) Add poppler's bin folder to your PATH**
```powershell
$bin = (Get-ChildItem "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\WinGet\Packages\oschwartz10612.Poppler*" -Recurse -Filter pdftoppm.exe | Select-Object -First 1).DirectoryName
$bin   # shows the folder — sanity check it printed a path
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("Path", [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path","User") + ";$bin", "User")
```
Then open *another* fresh PowerShell and confirm `pdftoppm -h` works.

**4. Fully restart Claude Code** (close and reopen the app/terminal). That's what lets my tools inherit the new PATH.

**5. Tell me "try the PDF now"** — I'll re-run the Read tool on `Dave Miller.pdf` and should get rendered page images.

---

Two notes so you can decide if it's even worth doing:
- **It changes nothing for the current work.** I already have 100% of the illustration's *content* via `pdftotext`, and Arlindo builds its own navy/gold deck rather than mimicking the carrier PDF's look — so Phase 1–3 don't need image rendering. This is purely a nice-to-have general capability.
- If you'd rather not restart mid-run, **do the install now but hold the restart until the three phases are done** — nothing I'm doing depends on it, and the deep-research agents are still working in the background regardless.

Want me to attempt the `winget install` myself instead? I can try, but if it throws a UAC or agreement prompt it'll stall, so your own terminal is the sure path.

message.content: Should I install this in every repo I have?

content.thinking: The user is asking whether poppler needs to be installed in each repository they work on. Since poppler is installed system-wide through winget and added to their PATH, it's available globally across all their projects — there's no need to install it per repository. This is different from project-level dependencies that get installed locally within each repo.

content.text:
No — you installed it **once, for your whole Windows user account**, not per-repo. It's done everywhere.

Here's the mental model:

- **winget installs are system/user-scoped.** Poppler went into your user profile and got added to your **user PATH** — exactly like `git`, `node`, or `gh`. Any terminal, any repo, any folder can now call `pdftoppm`. Installing it again in another repo would just be reinstalling the same global tool.

- **Per-repo installs are a different thing** — that's `npm install` / `node_modules`, Python venvs, etc. Those are project dependencies that live inside the project folder because each project pins its own versions. Poppler isn't that; it's a general-purpose command-line tool.

So: one install, you're covered globally. The only per-machine caveat is the one you already hit — a running program (like this Claude Code session) needs a restart to pick up the new PATH. New terminals/sessions started *after* the install already see it.

(If you ever set up a **fresh Windows machine**, you'd install it once there too. Same as you'd reinstall git.)

Nothing else to do — and the deep-research workflow is still running in the background; I'll continue Phase 1 as soon as it lands.

content.text:
Same idea, different installer — it's a **separate machine**, so you'd install it once there too (tools don't sync across machines). On macOS the package manager is Homebrew:

```bash
brew install poppler
```

That puts `pdftoppm`, `pdftotext`, and the rest into Homebrew's `bin` (already on your PATH — `/opt/homebrew/bin` on Apple Silicon, `/usr/local/bin` on Intel). Global for your user, every repo, just like the Windows install.

- **If you don't have Homebrew yet:** install it first from [brew.sh](https://brew.sh) (`/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"`), then run the `brew install` above.
- **Same restart caveat:** any already-open Claude Code / terminal needs reopening to see the new PATH; new ones pick it up automatically.

Quick verify after install: `pdftoppm -v` should print the version.

That's the whole story — Windows: `winget install oschwartz10612.Poppler`; Mac: `brew install poppler`; each machine once.

Still waiting on the deep-research workflow in the background; I'll jump back into Phase 1 as soon as it finishes.

48	    "summary": "National Life Group (NLG) and its subsidiary Life Insurance Company of the Southwest (LSW) market term life insurance as the \"Guaranteed Series\" — four fully-guaranteed level-premium contracts (G10, G15, G20, G30, current form era ICC18-20522) plus an Annual Renewable Term (ART) product; older LSW materials also expose 15-NG/20-NG/30-NG variants (10-year guarantee, current-level thereafter) and label the section \"Term LSW and Term NL Life.\" Term is level-benefit with no cash value and the lowest initial cost in the lineup; issue ages run 18 to 75 for shorter terms and shorten as term lengthens (30-year to 18-55 NT / 18-50 tobacco; ART to 18-85), with coverage on all plans extending to age 95. Rate classes are Elite (max age 75), Preferred/Select/Standard/Express NT and Preferred/Standard/Express tobacco (to age 85, Express capped at $2M face), and policies convert to permanent coverage with no evidence of insurability provided face is not increased and no riders are added. All term plans carry a no-additional-cost Accelerated Benefit Rider suite — Terminal, Chronic, Critical Illness, Critical Injury, and (added Jan 2023) Alzheimer's/Lewy Body Dementia — paying an actuarially discounted (not dollar-for-dollar) benefit, subject to per-insured lifetime maximums (Terminal $1.5M, Chronic $1.5M / $2M NY, Critical $1M, reduced to $500K in IL/NJ). Critically, term is NOT available to foreign nationals — NLG/LSW offers permanent products only to non-US-residents (A/B countries, $500K minimum / $15M maximum face), so Brazil (\"B\" country) applicants cannot buy term.",
