    "question": "National Life Group (NLG) / Life Insurance Company of the Southwest (LSW) TERM LIFE INSURANCE products — comprehensive product reference. Cover: (1) the full current term lineup and product names (e.g. the \"G\" guaranteed level-premium series Term 10-G / 15-G / 20-G / 30-G, form series ICC18-20522, and any other current term products like LSW Level Term / annually-renewable term); (2) issue ages and available level term lengths; (3) the level-premium period then annually-renewable-to-age-95 structure, whether the policy has cash value/dividends (it does not); (4) the conversion privilege — window (first 20 years or to age 70 if earlier), no evidence of insurability, equivalent rate class, what permanent products it converts to; (5) the Accelerated Benefit Rider (ABR) living-benefit suite available on term (Terminal, Chronic, Critical Illness, Critical Injury, Alzheimer's) — cost (no additional premium), triggers, discounted/condition-specific payout, and per-insured lifetime benefit limits and how they vary by state; (6) premium structure and rate classes (Elite/Preferred/Select/Standard/Express, tobacco/non-tobacco); (7) state availability variations; (8) foreign-national / non-US-resident rules for term (minimum face, US nexus/solicitation/delivery requirements, whether Brazil/\"B\" country is eligible, whether term is even offered to foreign nationals). Prioritize NLG/LSW primary sources: nationallife.com, the Life Product Quick Reference Chart, agent guides, product guides, rider brochures, and the Foreign National Guidelines. Distinguish guaranteed/structural facts from rate-sheet-driven current rates (which change often). Produce a cited report with source URLs.",
    "summary": "National Life Group (NLG) and its subsidiary Life Insurance Company of the Southwest (LSW) market term life insurance as the \"Guaranteed Series\" — four fully-guaranteed level-premium contracts (G10, G15, G20, G30, current form era ICC18-20522) plus an Annual Renewable Term (ART) product; older LSW materials also expose 15-NG/20-NG/30-NG variants (10-year guarantee, current-level thereafter) and label the section \"Term LSW and Term NL Life.\" Term is level-benefit with no cash value and the lowest initial cost in the lineup; issue ages run 18 to 75 for shorter terms and shorten as term lengthens (30-year to 18-55 NT / 18-50 tobacco; ART to 18-85), with coverage on all plans extending to age 95. Rate classes are Elite (max age 75), Preferred/Select/Standard/Express NT and Preferred/Standard/Express tobacco (to age 85, Express capped at $2M face), and policies convert to permanent coverage with no evidence of insurability provided face is not increased and no riders are added. All term plans carry a no-additional-cost Accelerated Benefit Rider suite — Terminal, Chronic, Critical Illness, Critical Injury, and (added Jan 2023) Alzheimer's/Lewy Body Dementia — paying an actuarially discounted (not dollar-for-dollar) benefit, subject to per-insured lifetime maximums (Terminal $1.5M, Chronic $1.5M / $2M NY, Critical $1M, reduced to $500K in IL/NJ). Critically, term is NOT available to foreign nationals — NLG/LSW offers permanent products only to non-US-residents (A/B countries, $500K minimum / $15M maximum face), so Brazil (\"B\" country) applicants cannot buy term.",
    "findings": [
      {
        "claim": "The current NLG/LSW term lineup is the 'Guaranteed Series' — four fully-guaranteed level-premium contracts branded G10, G15, G20, G30 (level premiums guaranteed for 10/15/20/30 years respectively) — plus a separate Annual Renewable Term (ART) product with premiums that increase annually. Older LSW product guides additionally expose 15-NG/20-NG/30-NG variants (current-level premiums with only a 10-year guarantee). The current marketing form series is ICC18-20522 ('Level Term xx-G'); the same products appear under the underwriting-document framing 'Term LSW and Term NL Life.'",
        "confidence": "high",
        "evidence": "The NLG Life Product Quick Reference Chart (item 63236, rev 10-25) states verbatim that the 'Guaranteed Series provides the longest guaranteed premium period through four level term life insurance contracts -- G10, G15, G20, G30' with premiums 'guaranteed to remain level for 10, 15, 20, or 30 years, respectively,' and describes ART as 'annually renewable term life insurance policy with guaranteed premiums that increase annually.' The 2013 LSW Level Term Product Guide describes 'seven level premium, level benefit term life products' — fully guaranteed 10/15/20/30-G plus 15/20/30-year plans with a 10-year guarantee (15-NG/20-NG/30-NG). The 2023 NLG Underwriting Guide contains a section 'Term LSW and Term NL Life' covering the 10/15/20/30-Year Term + ART lineup — the same products under a different document framing.",
        "sources": [

      {
        "claim": "Term products provide a level amount of coverage during the term period with NO cash value accumulation or dividends, and offer the lowest initial cost of all NLG products. The level-premium period is followed by an annually-renewable structure with coverage extending to the policy anniversary following the insured's 95th birthday.",
        "confidence": "high",
        "evidence": "The NLG Life Product Quick Reference Chart (form 63236, rev 10-25) states verbatim: 'Term offers the lowest initial cost of all our products and a level amount of life insurance coverage during the term period with no cash value accumulation.' The 2013 LSW Level Term Product Guide confirms on all four product pages that 'coverage under [this] plan extends to age 95' and 'the policy remains in force until the policy anniversary following the insured's 95th birthday.' No cash value is a structural feature of term, corroborated by the chart contrasting term against WL/IUL products that cite guaranteed cash-value growth.",
        "sources": [
          "https://www.fmiagent.com/wp-content/uploads/2025-11-12_National_Life_Group_Life_Product_Quick_Reference_Chart_63236_10-25.pdf",
          "http://theinsgroup.net/images/stories/LSW/Life/LSW_Level_Term_Product_Guide.pdf"

      },

        "claim": "Issue ages for level-term products start at age 18 and the maximum issue age shortens as the term lengthens: 10-G to age 75; 15-G/NG to 75 (18-70 tobacco); 20-G/NG to 70 (18-65 tobacco); 30-G/NG to 55 non-tobacco / 50 tobacco; ART issues 18-85.",
        "confidence": "high",
        "evidence": "The LSW Life Insurance Underwriting Guide product/issue-age table reads: 'Ages 18-75: 10-G ... Ages 18-75: 15-G/NG ... Ages 18-70: 20-G/NG ... Ages 18-55: 30G/NG.' The 2023 NLG Underwriting Guide 'ISSUE AGES OFFERED' block states: 'ART 18-85 / 10 Year Term 18-75 / 15 Year Term 18-75 Non-tobacco 18-70 Tobacco / 20 Year Term 18-70 Non-tobacco 18-65 Tobacco / 30 Year Term 18-55 Non-tobacco 18-50 Tobacco.' Two independent primary sources agree on the load-bearing figures.",
        "sources": [
          "https://www.totalfinancial.com/carriers/downloads/lsw/lsw-uw1.pdf",
          "http://www.cassaniinsurance.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/National-Life-Group-Underwriting-Guide-2023.pdf",

        "claim": "Term is offered across an Elite / Preferred / Select / Standard / Express rate-class structure split by tobacco use (Elite, Preferred NT, Select NT, Standard NT, Express Standard NT, Preferred Tobacco, Standard Tobacco, Express Standard Tobacco). Elite has a maximum issue age of 75; all other classes extend to 85. Express Standard classes are limited to total face amounts of $2,000,000 or less.",
        "confidence": "high",
        "evidence": "The 2023 NLG Underwriting Guide 'Term products rate class offerings' table lists exactly: Elite (All Face Amounts, 18-75), Preferred NT (18-85), Select NT (18-85), Standard NT (18-85), Express Standard NT 1 and 2 (Total Face <= $2,000,000, 18-85), Preferred Tobacco (18-85), Standard Tobacco (18-85), Express Standard Tobacco (Total Face <= $2,000,000, 18-85). Elite max age 75 vs 85 for others, and the $2M Express cap, are confirmed exactly. These are structural underwriting facts, not volatile rate-sheet figures.",

Conversion is to a single-life permanent plan then offered by the company, during a conversion window (e.g. first 15-20 years or to age 70, whichever is earlier).",
        "confidence": "high",
        "evidence": "The NLG Term2Perm ekit states verbatim: 'No underwriting required*' with the asterisk defined as '*Additional underwriting will be required if coverage amount is being increased or additional riders are being added.' Corroborated by the LSW Level Term 20-G specimen/product materials describing conversion 'without evidence of insurability to any single life permanent plan of life insurance then sold' during the conversion window (first 15 yrs or to age 70, whichever earlier).",
        "sources": [

        "vote": "3-0 (claim 5)"

      {
        "claim": "All NLG/LSW term plans include an Accelerated Benefit Rider (living-benefits) suite at NO additional premium cost. As of January 2023 the suite covers Terminal, Chronic, Critical Illness, Critical Injury, and Alzheimer's Disease / Lewy Body Dementia (the Alzheimer's/Lewy Body addition expanded the prior four-condition set effective Jan 28, 2023). NLG markets itself as the only major carrier including Critical Injury as a standard rider on term. A Children's Term rider and Waiver of Premium rider (which waives premium on the converted policy) are also available.",
        "confidence": "high",
        "evidence": "The NLG newsroom press release (Jan 30, 2023, effective Jan 28, 2023) states the suite provides protection for 'a qualifying terminal, chronic, critical illness, critical injury or a diagnosis of Alzheimer's Disease or Lewy Body Dementia,' available on all life products subject to state approval. The NLG Life Product Quick Reference Chart (10-25) lists in the term row: 'ABR Terminal, Chronic, Critical Illness, Critical Injury, Alzheimer's Disease and Lewy Body Dementia, Children's Term ... Conversion Privileges. Waiver of Premium Rider waives premium on converted policy.' The 2014 LSW Living Benefits guide confirms ABRs are 'no additional cost riders.' Corroborated by InsuranceNewsNet and Wink Intel trade press.",
        "sources": [
          "https://www.nationallife.com/Our-Story/newsroom/NLG-expand-suite-of-living-benefits-adds-Alzheimers-and-Fertility-Riders",
          "https://www.fmiagent.com/wp-content/uploads/2025-11-12_National_Life_Group_Life_Product_Quick_Reference_Chart_63236_10-25.pdf",

        "vote": "3-0 (merged from claims 3, 8, 9)"
      },
      {
        "claim": "The Accelerated Benefit Rider pays a discounted benefit — the actuarially discounted value of the death benefit being accelerated, less a pro-rata portion of any policy debt — NOT a dollar-for-dollar acceleration. The degree of discounting reflects the insured's life expectancy/age and, for the Critical Illness rider (ABR3), the severity of the qualifying condition; a per-election administrative fee (historically up to $500) may apply.",
        "confidence": "high",
        "evidence": "The NLG rider help page states the benefit is 'the actuarially discounted value of the death benefit being accelerated less a pro-rata portion of any policy debt.' The LSW 'Living Benefits' brochure states 'With ABR3, the level of discounting applied to the death benefit varies depending upon the insured's age and the severity of the critical illness' and 'the actual ABR payment you receive will be less than the death benefit you accelerate.' Multiple independent NLG/LSW product guides corroborate the discounted-value mechanism.",
        "sources": [
          "https://www.nationallife.com/NWI/Help/en-US/Peach/Riders_ChronicABR2_CriticalABR3.htm",

These limits vary by state — notably reduced to $500,000 in Illinois and New Jersey for terminal, chronic, and critical benefits.",
        "confidence": "high",
        "evidence": "The LSW Accelerated Benefits Riders Agent Guide states National Life 'limits the amount of death benefit that may be accelerated under all contracts made over the entire lifetime of the insured to $1,500,000 for terminal or chronic illness, and $1,000,000 for critical illness or injury,' with 'Chronic For NY Only: $2,000,000.' The $500,000 IL/NJ limit is independently confirmed in the same guide.",
        "sources": [
          "https://theinsgroup.net/images/stories/LSW/Life/LSW_Living_Benefits_Rider.pdf"

Eligibility is limited to citizens/residents of 'A' or 'B' classified countries (Brazil is a 'B' country, so eligible for permanent but not term; C countries get individual facultative consideration; D and E countries cannot be covered).",
        "confidence": "high",
        "evidence": "The current NLG Foreign National Guidelines (Cat No 69798, rev 11-25), page 4 Product/Life column, states verbatim: 'Permanent Products Only -- up to Class 4 (200%)' and 'RapidProtect/RapidProtect NL not available for Foreign Nationals.' Riders are 'ABRs Only -- A & B countries only'; 'In D & E countries, we cannot offer coverage.' Brazil is listed as a B country (p.7). The older totalfinancial.com LSW underwriting guide states 'Only permanent plans of insurance will be considered. No term insurance is eligible... Only citizens/residents of countries classified as A or B.' Independent web sources confirm NLG offers permanent-only to foreign nationals.",

NLG defines a foreign national as anyone (including US citizens living abroad) spending more than 4 months outside the US in a consecutive 12-month period, and requires significant documented US assets (minimum $500,000).",

    "caveats": "Time-sensitivity: Actual premium dollar amounts are rate-sheet-driven and change frequently — this report deliberately covers only structural/guaranteed facts (product names, issue ages, structure, rate-class labels, conversion terms, rider triggers and lifetime limits), not current rates. Document currency varies: the strongest structural sources range from the 2013 LSW Level Term Product Guide (form MK2303) and 2014 LSW Living Benefits guide (which predate the Critical Injury and Alzheimer's rider additions) through the 2023 NLG Underwriting Guide to the current Oct/Nov 2025 Quick Reference Chart and Foreign National Guidelines (69798, 11-25). Where a fact is scoped to a specific older document (e.g., the three-rider ABR set), that reflects that document's vintage, not the current offering; the current suite is the six-condition set (Terminal, Chronic, Critical Illness, Critical Injury, Alzheimer's, Lewy Body). Several primary PDFs are hosted on independent agent/brokerage websites (mirrors of genuine NLG/LSW forms) rather than nationallife.com directly, but form numbers and content match NLG's own materials. One widely-listed source (im_10743.pdf) was confirmed to be an American National document, not NLG/LSW, and was excluded.",

content.thinking:
I've got the complete research findings here—the result object with all the key data is intact. The research is well-verified with three confirmed findings at high confidence. The first major finding covers the product lineup, which includes a "Guaranteed Series" with four premium contract tiers (G10, G15, G20, G30) plus an ART option, with older documentation referencing slightly different naming conventions.

The remaining findings detail the structural characteristics—level coverage with no cash value or dividends at the lowest initial cost, followed by a level-premium period transitioning to ART at age 95. Issue ages range from 18–75 depending on the contract type, with the 30-G product capped at age 55 for non-tobacco and 50 for tobacco users. The rate classes span from Elite down through Standard and Express tiers, with Express policies limited to $2M face value. Conversion rights allow switching to single-life permanent plans without evidence of insurability, provided the face amount doesn't increase and no riders are added, with conversion windows typically spanning 15–20 years or to age 70 whichever comes first. The ABR suite includes terminal, chronic, critical illness, critical injury, and Alzheimer's/Lewy Body riders (the latter added in January 2023), plus children's term and waiver of premium options, with NLG positioning itself as the only major carrier offering critical injury as a standard feature. The ABR riders are available at no additional premium and carry actuarially discounted values.

The ABR limits cap terminal and chronic illness at $1.5M ($2M in New York), critical illness and injury at $1M, with reductions to $500k in Illinois and New Jersey. A critical distinction emerges for foreign nationals: term products are not available to them—only permanent plans up to Class 4 with 200% limits, excluding RapidProtect. Foreign nationals must be from A or B countries (Brazil qualifies as B), while D and E countries cannot be covered. This is the pivotal constraint for Arlindo's situation as a Brazil-based agent. For permanent coverage, foreign nationals face a $500k minimum and $15M maximum ($2M for students), requiring US nexus, US delivery, English-language forms, and documented US assets of at least $500k, with foreign national status defined as spending more than four months annually outside the US. I also need to flag that one source document is actually American National rather than NLG, so that should be excluded from the analysis.

Several claims didn't hold up under scrutiny: the "Guaranteed Series" branding is the correct terminology, not "Term LSW" or "Term NL Life"; the rate class structure with declining maximum ages per class; the ABR availability grid; and the idea of a single $1M aggregate cap across all riders—the per-condition limits are what actually apply. I couldn't verify the specific state exclusion lists for chronic and critical illness riders, so those shouldn't be relied upon. There are still some open questions around the current state-exclusion lists, whether the conversion window is 15 or 20 years, the exact form series per product, and the minimum face amount for US-resident term policies.

Now I have both the research report and Dave Miller's primary-source illustration to cross-reference, and they're aligning well so far—Dave Miller indicates conversion is available for the first 20 years or to age 70, which matches what the research uncovered. The term product isn't available to foreign nationals — a critical finding that directly impacts Arlindo's Brazil-based clients, meaning he can't offer this option to them despite it being a domestic US product. Dave Miller's Virginia residency confirms the term is actively used for US residents, so this foreign-national restriction is a key distinction that needs to be flagged in the knowledge base and UI to avoid incorrectly suggesting term eligibility for international clients.

Now I'm setting up an adversarial accuracy audit workflow for the term claims that mirrors the existing IUL audit structure — using the same format with evidence gathering, skeptical refutation, and synthesis against primary sources. The deep-research skill already does three-vote adversarial verification per claim, but the user wants a separate dedicated audit workflow that specifically validates the term KB claims I'm about to ship, following the same flexlife-audit JSON template with appSays, sourcesSay, evidence, recommendation, verdict, and sourceUrls fields.

I'm assembling a claim set of roughly 12-14 claims covering the key term product aspects: lineup, core structure and no-cash-value design, issue ages, rate classes, conversion options, the ABR suite, ABR discounting mechanics, ABR limits, foreign-national exclusion rules, permanent resident rules, premium modes from the sample data, term-versus-permanent positioning, and compliance disclaimers — all grounded in both my research findings and the Dave Miller primary source. Then I'll run the adversarial audit workflow against this claim set.

After that, I need to generate three outputs: a curated knowledge document with confidence levels, verdicts, and source URLs plus a corrections section; a raw JSON file with all the audit verdicts and synthesis results dated 2026-07-18; and an update to the README. For the workflow itself, I'm building a script that takes each claim and runs it through a verifier who gathers evidence from the primary sources, then a skeptic who challenges it, and finally a synthesis step that produces the final verdict.

The synthesis agent combines the verifier and skeptic perspectives into a structured verdict—marked as CONFIRMED, PARTIAL, or UNVERIFIABLE—with confidence scores, source statements, evidence, recommendations, and URLs. I'll collect all verdicts into the JSON output. Following the IUL audit pattern of roughly 2-3 agents per claim, I'm planning about 3 agents per claim (verifier, skeptic, synthesis), which for ~14 claims means around 42 agents total plus a final overall synthesis pass. That mirrors and exceeds the thoroughness of the IUL run.

Now I'm designing the workflow script with a metadata section defining the audit phases, a CLAIMS array with each claim's ID, area, and statement, then a pipeline that runs the verifier and skeptic in parallel for each claim to gather evidence and refutations, followed by a synthesis stage.

Each agent needs web access to validate claims against primary sources, so I'll pass the key source URLs discovered upfront to avoid redundant research. Now I need to define the schema for the verdict object that captures the synthesized judgment.

I'm starting to map out the specific claims about the term life products — grounding each one in the research and sample documents. The first claim covers the product lineup: National Life Group's LSW term life offering includes the Guaranteed Series with four level-premium contracts (10, 15, 20, and 30-year terms) plus an Annual Renewable Term option, all under form ICC18-20522, with the sample being a Term 30-G issued by LSW.

Now I'm detailing the structure of the term product itself — it provides a level death benefit with no cash value or dividends, making it the lowest-cost entry point for National Life Group, and after the guaranteed period ends, premiums increase annually with coverage extending to age 95.

I'm also capturing the issue age parameters, which vary by term length: the 10 and 15-year products go to age 75, the 20-year to 70, the 30-year to 55 for non-tobacco applicants and 50 for tobacco users, while ART covers ages 18 through 85, with all policies running through age 95.

Finally, I'm documenting the rate class structure across tobacco and non-tobacco categories, including Elite, Preferred, Select, Standard, and Express Standard tiers, with Express Standard classes having specific limitations on total face amounts.

The conversion feature allows policyholders to switch to a permanent plan without medical underwriting as long as they don't increase the face amount or add riders, typically within the first 20 years or to age 70, whichever comes first, though this window can vary by product and state.

All term plans come bundled with an Accelerated Benefit Rider suite at no extra cost, covering terminal illness, chronic conditions, critical illness, critical injury, and Alzheimer's or Lewy Body Dementia benefits.

NLG distinguishes itself by being the only major carrier that includes Critical Injury as a standard rider, and they also offer optional Children's Term and Waiver of Premium riders.

The ABR triggers are clearly defined: terminal illness means 24 months or less life expectancy, chronic conditions involve inability to perform two of six activities of daily living or cognitive decline, critical illness covers conditions like cancer, heart attack, stroke, and ALS, critical injury includes coma, paralysis, severe burns, and traumatic brain injury, and Alzheimer's has its own specific criteria.

The key distinction with NLG's ABR is that it pays a discounted benefit rather than the full death benefit amount—the discount reflects the insured's age, life expectancy, and for critical conditions, the severity level, which means the client receives substantially less than what's being accelerated, ranging from about 86% for terminal illness down to potentially nothing for critical injury depending on severity.

There are also per-insured lifetime maximums that cap how much can be accelerated across all contracts: $1.5M for terminal and chronic, $1M for critical illness/injury combined, and $1.5M for Alzheimer's, though these limits vary by state and the carrier can adjust them downward but not below $500k.

National Life Group doesn't offer term insurance to foreign nationals or non-US residents—only permanent products up to Class 4 are available for eligible countries, and RapidProtect is excluded entirely.

For permanent policies issued to foreign nationals, there's a $500k minimum face value with a $15M maximum ($2M for students), requiring documented US nexus, significant US assets of at least $500k, US delivery, and all sales activity conducted within the US using English-language forms.

A foreign national is defined as anyone spending more than four months outside the US in any consecutive twelve-month period. Term premiums can be paid annually, semi-annually, quarterly, or monthly via EFT, with more frequent payments costing more per year—in the sample, annual was $813 versus $858.48 yearly on monthly payments. Term's main advantage is the lowest initial cost and highest death benefit relative to premium, but it has no cash value and premiums rise steeply after the level-premium period ends. That gives me 14 comprehensive claims total, matching and exceeding what I outlined for IUL. Now I'm setting up the audit workflow with primary source URLs so anyone can verify these directly—pulling from the FMI agent resources, foreign national guidelines, and the LSW product documentation. I'm also pulling in the ABR help documentation, then setting up the workflow script with a claims array containing id, area, and appSays fields, plus a sources string for the primary URLs to inject into prompts. I'm designing a pipeline that runs verifier and skeptic agents in parallel for each claim, then synthesizes their outputs into a verdict, with a final synthesis agent that aggregates all verdicts and returns the audit metadata, individual verdicts, and overall synthesis.

Writing the JavaScript implementation... Writing the workflow script...

The deep-research is complete and high quality — 20/25 claims confirmed 3-0, with the critical finding that **NLG/LSW term is NOT sold to foreign nationals (permanent-only for Brazil/"B" countries)**, plus confirmation of the per-condition ABR limits, the Guaranteed Series lineup, issue ages, conversion, and rate classes. It cross-checks cleanly against the Dave Miller primary source. The 5 refuted claims and open questions are noted.

Now Phase 1, step 2: the **adversarial accuracy audit** as an ultracode Workflow — per claim, an evidence-gathering verifier + a skeptic told to refute, against NLG/LSW primary sources, then per-claim synthesis, mirroring the IUL audit. I'll audit the 14 discrete claims Arlindo's Term KB will assert (grounded in the research + the sample). Let me write the workflow script.

input.content:
export const meta = {
  name: 'term-accuracy-audit',
  description: 'Adversarial factual-accuracy audit of the NLG/LSW Term claims Arlindo will ship (per claim: evidence verifier + skeptic vs primary sources, then synthesize)',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Audit', detail: 'per claim: 1 evidence-gathering verifier + 1 adversarial skeptic (NLG/LSW primary sources)' },
    { title: 'Synthesize', detail: 'per-claim verdict, then an overall synthesis report' },
  ],
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// The claims Arlindo's Term knowledge base + Term product will assert.
// Each is grounded in (a) the deep-research pass and (b) the real Term 30-G
// sample illustration (Dave Miller.pdf, LSW form ICC18-20522). The audit tests
// each against NLG/LSW PRIMARY sources — exactly mirroring the FlexLife IUL audit.
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
const CLAIMS = [
  { id: 'term-lineup', area: 'product', appSays:
    "National Life Group / LSW term life is marketed as the 'Guaranteed Series': four fully-guaranteed level-premium contracts — Term 10-G, 15-G, 20-G, 30-G (premiums guaranteed level for 10/15/20/30 years respectively) — plus an Annual Renewable Term (ART) whose premiums increase annually. Current level-term marketing form series is ICC18-20522 ('Level Term xx-G'). Underwritten by Life Insurance Company of the Southwest (LSW) and National Life (NL). Older LSW guides also list 15-NG/20-NG/30-NG variants (guaranteed 10 years, current-level thereafter)." },
  { id: 'term-structure', area: 'product', appSays:
    "Term provides a level death benefit for the term period with NO cash value accumulation and no dividends, and is the lowest-initial-cost product in the NLG lineup. After the level-premium period the policy is annually renewable with premiums that increase annually, and coverage extends to the policy anniversary following the insured's 95th birthday." },
  { id: 'issue-ages', area: 'product', appSays:
    "Level-term issue ages start at 18 and the maximum issue age shortens as the level term lengthens: 10-G and 15-G to age 75; 20-G to age 70 (65 tobacco); 30-G to age 55 non-tobacco / 50 tobacco; ART issues 18-85. Coverage on all plans extends to age 95." },
  { id: 'rate-classes', area: 'product', appSays:
    "Term is offered across these rate classes: Elite (all face amounts, max issue age 75), Preferred Non-Tobacco, Select Non-Tobacco, Standard Non-Tobacco, Express Standard Non-Tobacco, Preferred Tobacco, Standard Tobacco, and Express Standard Tobacco (all to age 85). Express Standard classes are limited to total face amounts of $2,000,000 or less. The actual rate class is determined at underwriting." },
  { id: 'conversion', area: 'product', appSays:
    "A term policy is convertible to a single-life permanent plan of insurance then sold by the company, with NO evidence of insurability, provided the coverage amount is not increased and no additional riders are added. The conversion window in the current Term 30-G sample is the first 20 years from issue or to age 70 if earlier; the window can vary by product/state (some older specimens show 15 years). The new permanent policy is issued at an equivalent rate class regardless of changes in health." },
  { id: 'abr-suite', area: 'rider', appSays:
    "All term plans include the Accelerated Benefit Rider (ABR) living-benefit suite at NO additional premium: Terminal Illness, Chronic Illness, Critical Illness, Critical Injury, and Alzheimer's Disease / Lewy Body Dementia (the Alzheimer's/Lewy Body condition was added effective Jan 28, 2023). NLG markets itself as the only major carrier including Critical Injury as a standard no-cost rider. A Children's Term rider and a Waiver of Premium rider (which waives premium on the converted policy) are also available on term." },
  { id: 'abr-triggers', area: 'rider', appSays:
    "ABR triggers: Terminal Illness = a physician-certified illness expected to result in death in 24 months or less; Chronic Illness = certified unable to perform 2 of 6 activities of daily living (bathing, continence, dressing, eating, toileting, transferring) or cognitively impaired; Critical Illness = a qualifying event such as cancer, heart attack, stroke, ALS, major organ transplant, end-stage renal failure; Critical Injury = coma, paralysis, severe burns, traumatic brain injury; Alzheimer's = specialist diagnosis of an eligible dementia (e.g. MMSE score < 20/30 plus clinical impairment)." },
  { id: 'abr-discount', area: 'rider', appSays:
    "The ABR benefit is paid on a DISCOUNTED basis — the actuarially discounted value of the death benefit being accelerated, less a pro-rata portion of any policy debt — not dollar-for-dollar; the client receives less than the amount accelerated. The discount reflects the insured's age/life expectancy and, for Critical Illness/Injury, the severity of the condition assessed at four levels (the benefit could be as low as $0). In the Term 30-G sample, if fully accelerated the projected discounted values were roughly 86% of the $600,000 death benefit for Terminal, ~62% for Alzheimer's, and $0-79% for Critical Illness/Injury, while Chronic Illness pays a monthly benefit." },
  { id: 'abr-limits', area: 'rider', appSays:
    "Per-insured lifetime maximum accelerated-benefit limits apply across all contracts on the insured: $1,500,000 for terminal illness, $1,500,000 for chronic illness, $1,000,000 for critical illness and critical injury (combined), and $1,500,000 for Alzheimer's disease. Chronic is also subject to a monthly maximum. These limits vary by state (NY chronic $2,000,000; IL and NJ reduced to $500,000) and the carrier reserves the right to change them but never below $500,000." },
  { id: 'foreign-national', area: 'compliance', appSays:
    "National Life Group / LSW does NOT offer TERM insurance to foreign nationals / non-US-residents — only PERMANENT life products are eligible (up to Class 4 / 200% table rating), and RapidProtect is also excluded. Eligibility is limited to citizens/residents of 'A' or 'B' classified countries; Brazil is a 'B' country (eligible for permanent, not term). Therefore, for the agent's Brazil-based foreign-national clients, term is not an available product — term is for US-resident clients (the sample insured is a Virginia resident)." },
  { id: 'fn-permanent-rules', area: 'compliance', appSays:
    "Foreign-national PERMANENT policies carry a $500,000 minimum face amount and a $15,000,000 maximum ($2,000,000 for students), and require a documented US nexus and significant documented US assets, US delivery to a valid address in the state of issue, all solicitation and sales activity conducted within the US (no marketing or policy materials transmitted or delivered outside the US), and English-language application and policy forms. NLG defines a foreign national as anyone (including US citizens living abroad) spending more than 4 months in a consecutive 12-month period outside the US." },
  { id: 'premium-modes', area: 'product', appSays:
    "Term premium can be paid annually, semi-annually, quarterly, or monthly (EFT); paying more frequently than annually increases the total yearly cost. In the Term 30-G sample the annual-mode premium was $813.00 versus $858.48/year on monthly EFT ($71.54/month). The illustration ledger shows only Policy Year, Age, Guaranteed Contract Premium, and Guaranteed Death Benefit (no cash value column)." },
  { id: 'term-vs-permanent', area: 'product', appSays:
    "Compared with permanent insurance, term's advantages are the lowest initial cost and the highest immediate death benefit per premium dollar; its trade-offs are that it builds no cash value, its level-premium period is finite (premiums then rise sharply on the annually-renewable schedule to age 95), and it is temporary protection. The conversion privilege lets the client move to a permanent policy (which does build cash value) with no new underwriting." },
  { id: 'compliance-disclaimers', area: 'compliance', appSays:
    "A compliant term presentation must state: guarantees depend on the claims-paying ability of the issuing company; the product is not FDIC/NCUA insured, carries no bank/credit-union guarantee, and may lose value; the death benefit is generally income-tax-free under IRC §101(a)(1); accelerated benefits are paid at a discount, may be taxable, and may affect eligibility for public-assistance programs; the use of one benefit may reduce or eliminate other policy and rider benefits; riders are optional, may require additional premium, are subject to underwriting/exclusions and may not be available in all states; and the official Statement of Policy Cost and Benefit Information is the authoritative document." },
]

// Primary NLG/LSW sources surfaced by the deep-research pass — verifier & skeptic
// should prioritize these (and any other genuine NLG/LSW primary docs they find).
const SOURCES = [
  'NLG Life Product Quick Reference Chart, Cat 63236 (10-25): https://www.fmiagent.com/wp-content/uploads/2025-11-12_National_Life_Group_Life_Product_Quick_Reference_Chart_63236_10-25.pdf',
  'NLG Foreign National Guidelines, Cat 69798 (11-25): https://www.fmiagent.com/wp-content/uploads/2025-11-12_National_Life_Group_Foreign_National_Guidelines_69798_11-25.pdf',
  'LSW Level Term Product Guide: http://theinsgroup.net/images/stories/LSW/Life/LSW_Level_Term_Product_Guide.pdf',
  'LSW Living Benefits (ABR) Rider agent guide: https://theinsgroup.net/images/stories/LSW/Life/LSW_Living_Benefits_Rider.pdf',
  '2023 NLG/LSW Underwriting Guide: http://www.cassaniinsurance.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/National-Life-Group-Underwriting-Guide-2023.pdf',
  'LSW Underwriting Guide (totalfinancial): https://www.totalfinancial.com/carriers/downloads/lsw/lsw-uw1.pdf',
  'NLG Term2Perm conversion ekit: https://www.nationallife.com/docs/digital/ekit/Term2Perm/index.html',
  "NLG newsroom — Alzheimer's/Lewy Body + Fertility riders (Jan 2023): https://www.nationallife.com/Our-Story/newsroom/NLG-expand-suite-of-living-benefits-adds-Alzheimers-and-Fertility-Riders",
  'NLG ABR help (Chronic ABR2 / Critical ABR3 discount mechanics): https://www.nationallife.com/NWI/Help/en-US/Peach/Riders_ChronicABR2_CriticalABR3.htm',
].join('\n')

const SAMPLE = `Cross-check primary source (a real, current LSW illustration): "Term 30-G", form series ICC18-20522, issued by Life Insurance Company of the Southwest, prepared 2026-07-11 (Version 26.1 A). Insured male 39 Elite Non-Tobacco, Virginia, $600,000 face, $71.54/mo EFT (=$858.48/yr), 5 ABRs. It states verbatim: "annually renewable to age 95. Premiums are level for the first 30 years and increase annually thereafter to attained age 95. This policy has no cash value and no dividends are payable." Convertible "during the first 20 years from the date of issue or until age 70 if earlier, without evidence of insurability to any single life permanent plan." ABR limits stated as "$1,500,000 for terminal illness, $1,500,000 for chronic illness, $1,000,000 for critical illness and critical injury and $1,500,000 for alzheimer's disease ... never be less than $500,000 ... vary by state."`

const VERDICT_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: false,
  required: ['id', 'area', 'verdict', 'confidence', 'appSays', 'sourcesSay', 'evidence', 'recommendation', 'sourceUrls'],
  properties: {
    id: { type: 'string' },
    area: { type: 'string' },
    verdict: { type: 'string', enum: ['CONFIRMED', 'PARTIAL', 'UNVERIFIABLE'] },
    confidence: { type: 'string', enum: ['high', 'medium', 'low'] },
    appSays: { type: 'string', description: 'The claim as Arlindo would assert it (echo the input).' },
    sourcesSay: { type: 'string', description: 'What NLG/LSW primary sources actually say, with specifics/quotes.' },
    evidence: { type: 'string', description: 'How it was verified: which docs, quotes, any caveats, and how the skeptic\'s objections were resolved.' },
    recommendation: { type: 'string', description: 'Concrete guidance for the Term KB/product: keep as-is, reword, add caveat, or drop.' },
    sourceUrls: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
  },
}

const REPORT_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: false,
  required: ['headline', 'problemClaims', 'keyCorrections', 'notesForArlindo'],
  properties: {
    headline: { type: 'string' },
    problemClaims: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
    keyCorrections: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
    notesForArlindo: { type: 'string' },
  },
}

phase('Audit')

const verdicts = await pipeline(
  CLAIMS,
  // Stage 1 — run the evidence-gathering verifier and the adversarial skeptic concurrently.
  (claim) => parallel([
    () => agent(
      `You are an EVIDENCE-GATHERING VERIFIER auditing a factual claim Arlindo (an insurance-presentation app) will ship about National Life Group (NLG) / Life Insurance Company of the Southwest (LSW) TERM life insurance.\n\nCLAIM (area: ${claim.area}):\n"""${claim.appSays}"""\n\nYour job: find NLG/LSW PRIMARY sources that CONFIRM or CORRECT each element of the claim. Use WebSearch/WebFetch (load them via ToolSearch "select:WebSearch,WebFetch" if not already available). Prioritize these known primary sources, and fetch them:\n${SOURCES}\n\n${SAMPLE}\n\nReport: which specific elements are supported (with exact quotes + the source URL + the document's form number/date), which are wrong or stale, and any nuance the claim omits. Distinguish guaranteed/structural facts from rate-sheet-driven current rates. Be precise about dates/editions. Return a thorough evidence writeup (plain text).`,
      { label: `verify:${claim.id}`, phase: 'Audit' },
    ),
    () => agent(
      `You are an ADVERSARIAL SKEPTIC. Your goal is to REFUTE the following factual claim that Arlindo (an insurance-presentation app) intends to ship about National Life Group (NLG) / LSW TERM life insurance. Assume it may be wrong, stale, or overstated until primary sources prove otherwise.\n\nCLAIM (area: ${claim.area}):\n"""${claim.appSays}"""\n\nActively hunt for: outdated editions, product/state variations that contradict it, conflation of term with permanent (IUL) facts, numbers that come from superseded brochures, and overgeneralizations. Use WebSearch/WebFetch (load via ToolSearch "select:WebSearch,WebFetch" if needed) against NLG/LSW primary sources:\n${SOURCES}\n\n${SAMPLE}\n\nDefault to skepticism: if an element cannot be confirmed in a current primary source, say so. List every specific way the claim is wrong, imprecise, or unverifiable, with the source that contradicts it (URL + quote). If after genuine effort you cannot refute an element, say which elements survive refutation. Return a plain-text refutation writeup.`,
      { label: `refute:${claim.id}`, phase: 'Audit' },
    ),
  ]).then((pair) => ({ claim, evidence: pair[0], refutation: pair[1] })),
  // Stage 2 — synthesize a single verdict per claim, weighing evidence vs refutation.
  (r) => agent(
    `You are the SYNTHESIS JUDGE for one claim in Arlindo's NLG/LSW Term accuracy audit. Weigh the verifier's evidence against the skeptic's refutation and issue a final verdict.\n\nCLAIM (id: ${r.claim.id}, area: ${r.claim.area}):\n"""${r.claim.appSays}"""\n\n=== VERIFIER (evidence) ===\n${r.evidence || '(no evidence returned)'}\n\n=== SKEPTIC (refutation) ===\n${r.refutation || '(no refutation returned)'}\n\nDecide: CONFIRMED (matches a current NLG/LSW primary source), PARTIAL (core correct but a specific element is misstated/stale/omitted), or UNVERIFIABLE (not found in primary sources). Set confidence (high/medium/low). Write sourcesSay (what the sources actually say, with specifics), evidence (how you resolved it, incl. how the skeptic's objections were handled), a concrete recommendation for the Term KB/product (keep / reword / add caveat / drop), and the list of source URLs actually relied upon. Echo the claim into appSays. Set id="${r.claim.id}" and area="${r.claim.area}".`,
    { label: `synth:${r.claim.id}`, phase: 'Synthesize', schema: VERDICT_SCHEMA },
  ),
)

phase('Synthesize')

const clean = verdicts.filter(Boolean)
const problems = clean.filter((v) => v.verdict !== 'CONFIRMED')

const report = await agent(
  `You are writing the synthesis report for Arlindo's NLG/LSW TERM factual-accuracy adversarial audit. Here are the per-claim verdicts (JSON):\n\n${JSON.stringify(clean, null, 2)}\n\nWrite: a one-paragraph headline (how accurate is the Term claim set overall; how many CONFIRMED vs PARTIAL vs UNVERIFIABLE); problemClaims (short 'id — the issue' strings for every non-CONFIRMED claim); keyCorrections (the concrete fixes Arlindo must apply to the Term KB / Term product data / disclaimers, most important first — e.g. foreign-national term exclusion, per-condition ABR limits, conversion-window state variation); and notesForArlindo (guidance on what is guaranteed/structural vs rate-sheet-driven and must never be hard-coded, plus the single authoritative-document rule).`,
  { label: 'synthesis-report', phase: 'Synthesize', schema: REPORT_SCHEMA },
)

return {
  audit: 'Arlindo National Life / LSW Term factual-accuracy adversarial audit',
  date: '2026-07-18',
  method: 'ultracode workflow — per claim: 1 evidence-gathering verifier + 1 adversarial skeptic (web research on NLG/LSW primary sources), then a synthesis judge; plus an overall synthesis report',
  totalClaims: CLAIMS.length,
  confirmed: clean.filter((v) => v.verdict === 'CONFIRMED').length,
  partial: clean.filter((v) => v.verdict === 'PARTIAL').length,
  unverifiable: clean.filter((v) => v.verdict === 'UNVERIFIABLE').length,
  problemCount: problems.length,
  verdicts: clean,
  synthesis: report,
}

export const meta = {
  name: 'term-accuracy-audit',
  description: 'Adversarial factual-accuracy audit of the NLG/LSW Term claims Arlindo will ship (per claim: evidence verifier + skeptic vs primary sources, then synthesize)',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Audit', detail: 'per claim: 1 evidence-gathering verifier + 1 adversarial skeptic (NLG/LSW primary sources)' },
    { title: 'Synthesize', detail: 'per-claim verdict, then an overall synthesis report' },
  ],
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// The claims Arlindo's Term knowledge base + Term product will assert.
// Each is grounded in (a) the deep-research pass and (b) the real Term 30-G
// sample illustration (Dave Miller.pdf, LSW form ICC18-20522). The audit tests
// each against NLG/LSW PRIMARY sources — exactly mirroring the FlexLife IUL audit.
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
const CLAIMS = [
  { id: 'term-lineup', area: 'product', appSays:
    "National Life Group / LSW term life is marketed as the 'Guaranteed Series': four fully-guaranteed level-premium contracts — Term 10-G, 15-G, 20-G, 30-G (premiums guaranteed level for 10/15/20/30 years respectively) — plus an Annual Renewable Term (ART) whose premiums increase annually. Current level-term marketing form series is ICC18-20522 ('Level Term xx-G'). Underwritten by Life Insurance Company of the Southwest (LSW) and National Life (NL). Older LSW guides also list 15-NG/20-NG/30-NG variants (guaranteed 10 years, current-level thereafter)." },
  { id: 'term-structure', area: 'product', appSays:
    "Term provides a level death benefit for the term period with NO cash value accumulation and no dividends, and is the lowest-initial-cost product in the NLG lineup. After the level-premium period the policy is annually renewable with premiums that increase annually, and coverage extends to the policy anniversary following the insured's 95th birthday." },
  { id: 'issue-ages', area: 'product', appSays:
    "Level-term issue ages start at 18 and the maximum issue age shortens as the level term lengthens: 10-G and 15-G to age 75; 20-G to age 70 (65 tobacco); 30-G to age 55 non-tobacco / 50 tobacco; ART issues 18-85. Coverage on all plans extends to age 95." },
  { id: 'rate-classes', area: 'product', appSays:
    "Term is offered across these rate classes: Elite (all face amounts, max issue age 75), Preferred Non-Tobacco, Select Non-Tobacco, Standard Non-Tobacco, Express Standard Non-Tobacco, Preferred Tobacco, Standard Tobacco, and Express Standard Tobacco (all to age 85). Express Standard classes are limited to total face amounts of $2,000,000 or less. The actual rate class is determined at underwriting." },
  { id: 'conversion', area: 'product', appSays:
    "A term policy is convertible to a single-life permanent plan of insurance then sold by the company, with NO evidence of insurability, provided the coverage amount is not increased and no additional riders are added. The conversion window in the current Term 30-G sample is the first 20 years from issue or to age 70 if earlier; the window can vary by product/state (some older specimens show 15 years). The new permanent policy is issued at an equivalent rate class regardless of changes in health." },
  { id: 'abr-suite', area: 'rider', appSays:
    "All term plans include the Accelerated Benefit Rider (ABR) living-benefit suite at NO additional premium: Terminal Illness, Chronic Illness, Critical Illness, Critical Injury, and Alzheimer's Disease / Lewy Body Dementia (the Alzheimer's/Lewy Body condition was added effective Jan 28, 2023). NLG markets itself as the only major carrier including Critical Injury as a standard no-cost rider. A Children's Term rider and a Waiver of Premium rider (which waives premium on the converted policy) are also available on term." },
  { id: 'abr-triggers', area: 'rider', appSays:
    "ABR triggers: Terminal Illness = a physician-certified illness expected to result in death in 24 months or less; Chronic Illness = certified unable to perform 2 of 6 activities of daily living (bathing, continence, dressing, eating, toileting, transferring) or cognitively impaired; Critical Illness = a qualifying event such as cancer, heart attack, stroke, ALS, major organ transplant, end-stage renal failure; Critical Injury = coma, paralysis, severe burns, traumatic brain injury; Alzheimer's = specialist diagnosis of an eligible dementia (e.g. MMSE score < 20/30 plus clinical impairment)." },
  { id: 'abr-discount', area: 'rider', appSays:
    "The ABR benefit is paid on a DISCOUNTED basis — the actuarially discounted value of the death benefit being accelerated, less a pro-rata portion of any policy debt — not dollar-for-dollar; the client receives less than the amount accelerated. The discount reflects the insured's age/life expectancy and, for Critical Illness/Injury, the severity of the condition assessed at four levels (the benefit could be as low as $0). In the Term 30-G sample, if fully accelerated the projected discounted values were roughly 86% of the $600,000 death benefit for Terminal, ~62% for Alzheimer's, and $0-79% for Critical Illness/Injury, while Chronic Illness pays a monthly benefit." },
  { id: 'abr-limits', area: 'rider', appSays:
    "Per-insured lifetime maximum accelerated-benefit limits apply across all contracts on the insured: $1,500,000 for terminal illness, $1,500,000 for chronic illness, $1,000,000 for critical illness and critical injury (combined), and $1,500,000 for Alzheimer's disease. Chronic is also subject to a monthly maximum. These limits vary by state (NY chronic $2,000,000; IL and NJ reduced to $500,000) and the carrier reserves the right to change them but never below $500,000." },
  { id: 'foreign-national', area: 'compliance', appSays:
    "National Life Group / LSW does NOT offer TERM insurance to foreign nationals / non-US-residents — only PERMANENT life products are eligible (up to Class 4 / 200% table rating), and RapidProtect is also excluded. Eligibility is limited to citizens/residents of 'A' or 'B' classified countries; Brazil is a 'B' country (eligible for permanent, not term). Therefore, for the agent's Brazil-based foreign-national clients, term is not an available product — term is for US-resident clients (the sample insured is a Virginia resident)." },
  { id: 'fn-permanent-rules', area: 'compliance', appSays:
    "Foreign-national PERMANENT policies carry a $500,000 minimum face amount and a $15,000,000 maximum ($2,000,000 for students), and require a documented US nexus and significant documented US assets, US delivery to a valid address in the state of issue, all solicitation and sales activity conducted within the US (no marketing or policy materials transmitted or delivered outside the US), and English-language application and policy forms. NLG defines a foreign national as anyone (including US citizens living abroad) spending more than 4 months in a consecutive 12-month period outside the US." },
  { id: 'premium-modes', area: 'product', appSays:
    "Term premium can be paid annually, semi-annually, quarterly, or monthly (EFT); paying more frequently than annually increases the total yearly cost. In the Term 30-G sample the annual-mode premium was $813.00 versus $858.48/year on monthly EFT ($71.54/month). The illustration ledger shows only Policy Year, Age, Guaranteed Contract Premium, and Guaranteed Death Benefit (no cash value column)." },
  { id: 'term-vs-permanent', area: 'product', appSays:
    "Compared with permanent insurance, term's advantages are the lowest initial cost and the highest immediate death benefit per premium dollar; its trade-offs are that it builds no cash value, its level-premium period is finite (premiums then rise sharply on the annually-renewable schedule to age 95), and it is temporary protection. The conversion privilege lets the client move to a permanent policy (which does build cash value) with no new underwriting." },
  { id: 'compliance-disclaimers', area: 'compliance', appSays:
    "A compliant term presentation must state: guarantees depend on the claims-paying ability of the issuing company; the product is not FDIC/NCUA insured, carries no bank/credit-union guarantee, and may lose value; the death benefit is generally income-tax-free under IRC §101(a)(1); accelerated benefits are paid at a discount, may be taxable, and may affect eligibility for public-assistance programs; the use of one benefit may reduce or eliminate other policy and rider benefits; riders are optional, may require additional premium, are subject to underwriting/exclusions and may not be available in all states; and the official Statement of Policy Cost and Benefit Information is the authoritative document." },
]

// Primary NLG/LSW sources surfaced by the deep-research pass — verifier & skeptic
// should prioritize these (and any other genuine NLG/LSW primary docs they find).
const SOURCES = [
  'NLG Life Product Quick Reference Chart, Cat 63236 (10-25): https://www.fmiagent.com/wp-content/uploads/2025-11-12_National_Life_Group_Life_Product_Quick_Reference_Chart_63236_10-25.pdf',
  'NLG Foreign National Guidelines, Cat 69798 (11-25): https://www.fmiagent.com/wp-content/uploads/2025-11-12_National_Life_Group_Foreign_National_Guidelines_69798_11-25.pdf',
  'LSW Level Term Product Guide: http://theinsgroup.net/images/stories/LSW/Life/LSW_Level_Term_Product_Guide.pdf',
  'LSW Living Benefits (ABR) Rider agent guide: https://theinsgroup.net/images/stories/LSW/Life/LSW_Living_Benefits_Rider.pdf',
  '2023 NLG/LSW Underwriting Guide: http://www.cassaniinsurance.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/National-Life-Group-Underwriting-Guide-2023.pdf',
  'LSW Underwriting Guide (totalfinancial): https://www.totalfinancial.com/carriers/downloads/lsw/lsw-uw1.pdf',
  'NLG Term2Perm conversion ekit: https://www.nationallife.com/docs/digital/ekit/Term2Perm/index.html',
  "NLG newsroom — Alzheimer's/Lewy Body + Fertility riders (Jan 2023): https://www.nationallife.com/Our-Story/newsroom/NLG-expand-suite-of-living-benefits-adds-Alzheimers-and-Fertility-Riders",
  'NLG ABR help (Chronic ABR2 / Critical ABR3 discount mechanics): https://www.nationallife.com/NWI/Help/en-US/Peach/Riders_ChronicABR2_CriticalABR3.htm',
].join('\n')

const SAMPLE = `Cross-check primary source (a real, current LSW illustration): "Term 30-G", form series ICC18-20522, issued by Life Insurance Company of the Southwest, prepared 2026-07-11 (Version 26.1 A). Insured male 39 Elite Non-Tobacco, Virginia, $600,000 face, $71.54/mo EFT (=$858.48/yr), 5 ABRs. It states verbatim: "annually renewable to age 95. Premiums are level for the first 30 years and increase annually thereafter to attained age 95. This policy has no cash value and no dividends are payable." Convertible "during the first 20 years from the date of issue or until age 70 if earlier, without evidence of insurability to any single life permanent plan." ABR limits stated as "$1,500,000 for terminal illness, $1,500,000 for chronic illness, $1,000,000 for critical illness and critical injury and $1,500,000 for alzheimer's disease ... never be less than $500,000 ... vary by state."`

const VERDICT_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: false,
  required: ['id', 'area', 'verdict', 'confidence', 'appSays', 'sourcesSay', 'evidence', 'recommendation', 'sourceUrls'],
  properties: {
    id: { type: 'string' },
    area: { type: 'string' },
    verdict: { type: 'string', enum: ['CONFIRMED', 'PARTIAL', 'UNVERIFIABLE'] },
    confidence: { type: 'string', enum: ['high', 'medium', 'low'] },
    appSays: { type: 'string', description: 'The claim as Arlindo would assert it (echo the input).' },
    sourcesSay: { type: 'string', description: 'What NLG/LSW primary sources actually say, with specifics/quotes.' },
    evidence: { type: 'string', description: 'How it was verified: which docs, quotes, any caveats, and how the skeptic\'s objections were resolved.' },
    recommendation: { type: 'string', description: 'Concrete guidance for the Term KB/product: keep as-is, reword, add caveat, or drop.' },
    sourceUrls: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
  },
}

const REPORT_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: false,
  required: ['headline', 'problemClaims', 'keyCorrections', 'notesForArlindo'],
  properties: {
    headline: { type: 'string' },
    problemClaims: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
    keyCorrections: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
    notesForArlindo: { type: 'string' },
  },
}

phase('Audit')

const verdicts = await pipeline(
  CLAIMS,
  // Stage 1 — run the evidence-gathering verifier and the adversarial skeptic concurrently.
  (claim) => parallel([
    () => agent(
      `You are an EVIDENCE-GATHERING VERIFIER auditing a factual claim Arlindo (an insurance-presentation app) will ship about National Life Group (NLG) / Life Insurance Company of the Southwest (LSW) TERM life insurance.\n\nCLAIM (area: ${claim.area}):\n"""${claim.appSays}"""\n\nYour job: find NLG/LSW PRIMARY sources that CONFIRM or CORRECT each element of the claim. Use WebSearch/WebFetch (load them via ToolSearch "select:WebSearch,WebFetch" if not already available). Prioritize these known primary sources, and fetch them:\n${SOURCES}\n\n${SAMPLE}\n\nReport: which specific elements are supported (with exact quotes + the source URL + the document's form number/date), which are wrong or stale, and any nuance the claim omits. Distinguish guaranteed/structural facts from rate-sheet-driven current rates. Be precise about dates/editions. Return a thorough evidence writeup (plain text).`,
      { label: `verify:${claim.id}`, phase: 'Audit' },
    ),
    () => agent(
      `You are an ADVERSARIAL SKEPTIC. Your goal is to REFUTE the following factual claim that Arlindo (an insurance-presentation app) intends to ship about National Life Group (NLG) / LSW TERM life insurance. Assume it may be wrong, stale, or overstated until primary sources prove otherwise.\n\nCLAIM (area: ${claim.area}):\n"""${claim.appSays}"""\n\nActively hunt for: outdated editions, product/state variations that contradict it, conflation of term with permanent (IUL) facts, numbers that come from superseded brochures, and overgeneralizations. Use WebSearch/WebFetch (load via ToolSearch "select:WebSearch,WebFetch" if needed) against NLG/LSW primary sources:\n${SOURCES}\n\n${SAMPLE}\n\nDefault to skepticism: if an element cannot be confirmed in a current primary source, say so. List every specific way the claim is wrong, imprecise, or unverifiable, with the source that contradicts it (URL + quote). If after genuine effort you cannot refute an element, say which elements survive refutation. Return a plain-text refutation writeup.`,
      { label: `refute:${claim.id}`, phase: 'Audit' },
    ),
  ]).then((pair) => ({ claim, evidence: pair[0], refutation: pair[1] })),
  // Stage 2 — synthesize a single verdict per claim, weighing evidence vs refutation.
  (r) => agent(
    `You are the SYNTHESIS JUDGE for one claim in Arlindo's NLG/LSW Term accuracy audit. Weigh the verifier's evidence against the skeptic's refutation and issue a final verdict.\n\nCLAIM (id: ${r.claim.id}, area: ${r.claim.area}):\n"""${r.claim.appSays}"""\n\n=== VERIFIER (evidence) ===\n${r.evidence || '(no evidence returned)'}\n\n=== SKEPTIC (refutation) ===\n${r.refutation || '(no refutation returned)'}\n\nDecide: CONFIRMED (matches a current NLG/LSW primary source), PARTIAL (core correct but a specific element is misstated/stale/omitted), or UNVERIFIABLE (not found in primary sources). Set confidence (high/medium/low). Write sourcesSay (what the sources actually say, with specifics), evidence (how you resolved it, incl. how the skeptic's objections were handled), a concrete recommendation for the Term KB/product (keep / reword / add caveat / drop), and the list of source URLs actually relied upon. Echo the claim into appSays. Set id="${r.claim.id}" and area="${r.claim.area}".`,
    { label: `synth:${r.claim.id}`, phase: 'Synthesize', schema: VERDICT_SCHEMA },
  ),
)

phase('Synthesize')

const clean = verdicts.filter(Boolean)
const problems = clean.filter((v) => v.verdict !== 'CONFIRMED')

const report = await agent(
  `You are writing the synthesis report for Arlindo's NLG/LSW TERM factual-accuracy adversarial audit. Here are the per-claim verdicts (JSON):\n\n${JSON.stringify(clean, null, 2)}\n\nWrite: a one-paragraph headline (how accurate is the Term claim set overall; how many CONFIRMED vs PARTIAL vs UNVERIFIABLE); problemClaims (short 'id — the issue' strings for every non-CONFIRMED claim); keyCorrections (the concrete fixes Arlindo must apply to the Term KB / Term product data / disclaimers, most important first — e.g. foreign-national term exclusion, per-condition ABR limits, conversion-window state variation); and notesForArlindo (guidance on what is guaranteed/structural vs rate-sheet-driven and must never be hard-coded, plus the single authoritative-document rule).`,
  { label: 'synthesis-report', phase: 'Synthesize', schema: REPORT_SCHEMA },
)

return {
  audit: 'Arlindo National Life / LSW Term factual-accuracy adversarial audit',
  date: '2026-07-18',
  method: 'ultracode workflow — per claim: 1 evidence-gathering verifier + 1 adversarial skeptic (web research on NLG/LSW primary sources), then a synthesis judge; plus an overall synthesis report',
  totalClaims: CLAIMS.length,
  confirmed: clean.filter((v) => v.verdict === 'CONFIRMED').length,
  partial: clean.filter((v) => v.verdict === 'PARTIAL').length,
  unverifiable: clean.filter((v) => v.verdict === 'UNVERIFIABLE').length,
  problemCount: problems.length,
  verdicts: clean,
  synthesis: report,
}

348	const SLIDE_COPY: Record<PresentationLanguage, SlideCopy> = { pt, en, es }
349	
350	export function slideCopy(lang: PresentationLanguage | undefined): SlideCopy {
351	  return SLIDE_COPY[lang ?? 'pt']
352	}
353	
354	/** Localized default product name (used when no custom title is set). */
355	export function defaultProductNameFor(lang: PresentationLanguage | undefined): string {
356	  return lang === 'en'
357	    ? 'Indexed Universal Life Insurance (IUL)'
358	    : lang === 'es'
359	      ? 'Seguro de Vida Universal Indexado (IUL)'
360	      : 'Seguro de Vida Universal Indexado (IUL)'
361	}
362	
363	/** Self-labeling note the estimate engine prepends, per language. */
364	export function estimateNoteFor(lang: PresentationLanguage | undefined): string {
365	  return lang === 'en'
366	    ? 'Estimate generated by the app (not guaranteed) — for illustration only. The insurer’s official illustration is the governing document.'
367	    : lang === 'es'
368	      ? 'Estimación generada por la aplicación (no garantizada) — solo para simulación. La ilustración oficial de la aseguradora es el documento válido.'
369	      : 'Estimativa gerada pelo aplicativo (não garantida) — apenas para simulação. A ilustração oficial da seguradora é o documento válido.'
370	}
371	
372	/** Rider label/note translations keyed by stable rider id (pt lives in @domain/model/riders). */
373	const RIDER_I18N: Record<'en' | 'es', Record<string, { label: string; note: string }>> = {
374	  en: {
375	    terminal_illness: { label: 'Terminal Illness', note: "No cost. Discounted acceleration for a terminal illness (life expectancy of up to 24 months). $1.5M lifetime limit shared with Chronic Illness and Alzheimer's." },
376	    chronic_illness: { label: 'Chronic Illness', note: "No cost. Inability to perform 2 of 6 activities of daily living for 90 days, or severe cognitive impairment. Up to 2%/month. $1.5M lifetime limit shared with Terminal Illness and Alzheimer's." },
377	    critical_illness: { label: 'Critical Illness', note: 'No cost. Cancer, stroke, heart attack, ALS, transplant and others. $1M lifetime limit (shared with Critical Injury).' },
378	    critical_injury: { label: 'Critical Injury / Accidents', note: 'No cost. Coma, paralysis, severe burns, traumatic brain injury. $1M lifetime limit (shared with Critical Illness).' },
379	    alzheimers: { label: "Alzheimer's", note: "No cost. Diagnosis of eligible Alzheimer's/dementia by a specialist. $1.5M lifetime limit shared with Terminal and Chronic Illness." },
380	    value_added_services: { label: 'Caregiver Support', note: 'No cost. Practical and emotional caregiver support (Homethrive). Not available in NY.' },
381	    fertility_journey: { label: 'Fertility Journey Support', note: 'No cost. Accumulated-value credit after eligible fertility treatment.' },
382	    premium_chronic_care: { label: 'Premium Chronic Care', note: 'Additional cost. Accelerates a larger share — up to the full death benefit (~$3M limit), 2% or 4% per month (chosen at issue). Not available in CA or NY.' },
383	    waiver_monthly_deductions: { label: 'Waiver of Monthly Deductions', note: 'Additional cost. Waives monthly deductions in case of total disability (~6-month waiting period / LSW).' },
384	    childrens_term: { label: "Children's Term", note: 'Additional cost. Coverage of $5,000 to $25,000 per child (in $1,000 increments), to age 25; single rate regardless of the number of children.' },
385	  },
386	  es: {
387	    terminal_illness: { label: 'Enfermedad Terminal', note: 'Sin costo. Anticipación con descuento en caso de enfermedad terminal (expectativa de hasta 24 meses). Límite vitalicio de US$ 1,5 M compartido con Enfermedad Crónica y Alzheimer.' },
388	    chronic_illness: { label: 'Enfermedad Crónica', note: 'Sin costo. Incapacidad de realizar 2 de 6 actividades diarias por 90 días, o deterioro cognitivo severo. Hasta 2%/mes. Límite vitalicio de US$ 1,5 M compartido con Enfermedad Terminal y Alzheimer.' },
389	    critical_illness: { label: 'Enfermedad Crítica', note: 'Sin costo. Cáncer, ACV, infarto, ELA, trasplante y otras. Límite vitalicio US$ 1 M (compartido con Lesión Crítica).' },
390	    critical_injury: { label: 'Lesión Crítica / Accidentes', note: 'Sin costo. Coma, parálisis, quemaduras graves, traumatismo craneal. Límite vitalicio US$ 1 M (compartido con Enfermedad Crítica).' },
391	    alzheimers: { label: 'Alzheimer', note: 'Sin costo. Diagnóstico de Alzheimer/demencia elegible por un especialista. Límite vitalicio de US$ 1,5 M compartido con Enfermedad Terminal y Crónica.' },
392	    value_added_services: { label: 'Apoyo al Cuidador', note: 'Sin costo. Apoyo práctico y emocional al cuidador (Homethrive). No disponible en NY.' },
393	    fertility_journey: { label: 'Apoyo al Camino de Fertilidad', note: 'Sin costo. Crédito al valor acumulado tras un tratamiento de fertilidad elegible.' },
394	    premium_chronic_care: { label: 'Cuidado Crónico Premium', note: 'Costo adicional. Anticipa una porción mayor — hasta el beneficio por muerte completo (límite ~US$ 3 M), 2% o 4% por mes (elegido en la emisión). No disponible en CA ni NY.' },
395	    waiver_monthly_deductions: { label: 'Exención de Costos por Invalidez', note: 'Costo adicional. Exime las deducciones mensuales en caso de invalidez total (carencia ~6 meses / LSW).' },
396	    childrens_term: { label: 'Seguro Temporal para Hijos', note: 'Costo adicional. Cobertura de US$ 5 mil a US$ 25 mil por hijo (múltiplos de US$ 1.000), hasta los 25 años; tarifa única independiente del número de hijos.' },
397	  },
398	}
399	
400	/** Canonical disclaimer sets for en/es (pt stays the stored/edited set). */
401	const DISCLAIMERS_I18N: Record<'en' | 'es', string[]> = {
402	  en: [
403	    'This is an illustration only and is not intended to predict actual performance. Refer to the policy for full details; in case of conflict, the policy controls.',
404	    'Non-guaranteed values are subject to change and may be higher or lower than illustrated.',
405	    'Using a living benefit may reduce or eliminate other policy and rider benefits.',
406	    'Riders are optional and may require an additional premium; they are subject to underwriting, exclusions and limitations, and may not be available in all states.',
407	    'The lifetime limits of the accelerated benefits are shared by rider group and per insured, and vary by state (for example: NY $2M; IL and NJ $500k).',
408	    'Guarantees depend on the claims-paying ability of the issuing company (National Life Insurance Company / Life Insurance Company of the Southwest).',
409	    'The maximum illustrated rate follows Actuarial Guideline 49-B. Past index performance does not represent future performance.',
410	    'Policy denominated in U.S. dollars (USD). Solicitation, illustration, signing and delivery must take place in the United States. The client is responsible for confirming that Brazilian law permits ownership of this policy.',
411	  ],
412	  es: [
413	    'Esta es solo una ilustración y no pretende predecir el desempeño real. Consulte la póliza para los detalles completos; en caso de conflicto, prevalece la póliza.',
414	    'Los valores no garantizados están sujetos a cambios y pueden ser mayores o menores que los ilustrados.',
415	    'El uso de un beneficio en vida puede reducir o eliminar otros beneficios de la póliza y de los riders.',
416	    'Los riders son opcionales y pueden requerir una prima adicional; están sujetos a suscripción, exclusiones y limitaciones, y pueden no estar disponibles en todos los estados.',
417	    'Los límites vitalicios de los beneficios acelerados se comparten por grupo de rider y por asegurado, y varían por estado (por ejemplo: NY US$ 2 M; IL y NJ US$ 500 mil).',
418	    'Las garantías dependen de la capacidad de pago de siniestros de la compañía emisora (National Life Insurance Company / Life Insurance Company of the Southwest).',
419	    'La tasa máxima ilustrada sigue la Actuarial Guideline 49-B. El desempeño pasado de los índices no representa el desempeño futuro.',
420	    'Póliza en dólares (US$). La solicitud, la ilustración, la firma y la entrega deben realizarse en los Estados Unidos. El cliente es responsable de confirmar que la legislación brasileña permite la titularidad de esta póliza.',
421	  ],
422	}
423	
424	/** Translate a rider's label/note into `lang` by stable id; falls back to the stored text. */
425	export function translateRider<T extends { id: string; label: string; note?: string }>(
426	  rider: T,
427	  lang: PresentationLanguage | undefined,
428	): T {
429	  if (lang == null || lang === 'pt') return rider
430	  const t = RIDER_I18N[lang]?.[rider.id]
431	  if (!t) return rider
432	  return { ...rider, label: t.label, note: rider.note != null ? t.note : rider.note }
433	}
434	
435	/** Disclaimers for a language: en/es use the canonical set; pt uses the stored one. */
436	export function disclaimersFor(
437	  lang: PresentationLanguage | undefined,
438	  ptFallback: string[],
439	): string[] {
440	  if (lang === 'en' || lang === 'es') return DISCLAIMERS_I18N[lang]
441	  return ptFallback
442	}
443

/**
 * All client-facing presentation copy, in the three output languages (pt/en/es).
 * The app UI stays Portuguese; only the generated slides / PDF / PPTX use this,
 * keyed by `presentationLanguage`. Rider names + disclaimers are compliance-
 * sensitive — keep translations faithful. Currency stays USD (locale only changes
 * grouping/symbol, handled in @domain/format via localeFor).
 */
export type PresentationLanguage = 'pt' | 'en' | 'es'

export interface SlideCopy {
  cover: { subtitle: string; clientFallback: string; agentFallback: string }
  explainer: {
    eyebrow: string
    title: string
    intro: (name: string) => { pre: string; strong: string; post: string }
    pillars: Array<{ title: string; body: string }>
  }
  coverage: {
    eyebrow: string
    title: string
    deposit: string
    perMonth: string
    perYear: string
    duringYears: (n: number) => string
    death: string
    livingLabel: (pct: string | null) => string
    includedTitle: string
    additionalCost: string
    upTo: (pct: string) => string
  }
  projection: {
    eyebrow: string
    title: string
    projectedLabel: (years: number | null) => string
    sub: string
    empty: string
  }
  table: {
    eyebrow: string
    title: string
    year: string
    age: string
    premium: string
    accumulated: string
    death: string
  }
  options: {
    eyebrow: string
    title: string
    opt1: string
    opt1Body: string
    opt2: string
    incomeForLife: string
    illustratedToAge: (age: number) => string
    perYear: string
    none: string
  }
  comparison: {
    eyebrow: string
    title: string
    term: string
    iul: string
    rows: Array<{ label: string; term: string; iul: string }>
  }
  disclaimers: { eyebrow: string; title: string }
  headline: {
    eyebrow: string
    title: string
    whenEarly: string
    whenLong: string
    whenIll: string
    labelDeath: string
    labelIncome: string
    labelLiving: string
    subDeath: string
    illustratedToAge: (age: number) => string
    livingUpToFull: string
    livingEarly: string
    livingUpTo: (money: string) => string
  }
  clientFallback: string
}

const pt: SlideCopy = {
  cover: { subtitle: 'Proposta Personalizada', clientFallback: 'Cliente', agentFallback: 'Agente' },
  explainer: {
    eyebrow: 'Entendendo o Produto',
    title: 'O que é uma IUL?',
    intro: (name) => ({
      pre: `A IUL é permanente: protege ${name} para sempre e, ao mesmo tempo, constrói um patrimônio que rende com base em um índice de mercado — `,
      strong: 'sobe com o índice, mas nunca cai quando o mercado cai',
      post: ' (existe um piso de proteção).',
    }),
    pillars: [
      { title: 'Proteção por Morte', body: 'Cobertura que protege a família para sempre.' },
      { title: 'Benefício em Vida', body: 'Antecipe parte do valor em caso de doença grave.' },
      { title: 'Valor Acumulado', body: 'Uma parte de cada depósito rende ao longo do tempo.' },
      { title: 'Sem Expiração', body: 'Proteção vitalícia — não expira como o seguro temporário.' },
    ],
  },
  coverage: {
    eyebrow: 'Sua Cobertura',
    title: 'Estrutura do Plano',
    deposit: 'Depósito',
    perMonth: '/mês',
    perYear: '/ano',
    duringYears: (n) => `durante ${n} anos`,
    death: 'Proteção por Morte',
    livingLabel: (pct) => `Benefício em Vida${pct ? ` (até ${pct})` : ''}`,
    includedTitle: 'Benefícios em Vida inclusos',
    additionalCost: '(custo adicional)',
    upTo: (pct) => `até ${pct}`,
  },
  projection: {
    eyebrow: 'Projeção de Longo Prazo',
    title: 'Crescimento do Valor Acumulado',
    projectedLabel: (years) => `Valor projetado${years ? ` em ${years} anos` : ''}`,
    sub: 'Disponível para resgate ou para continuar rendendo.',
    empty: 'Adicione a tabela ano a ano para ver o gráfico.',
  },
  table: {
    eyebrow: 'Detalhamento',
    title: 'Projeção Ano a Ano',
    year: 'Ano',
    age: 'Idade',
    premium: 'Depósito',
    accumulated: 'Valor acumulado',
    death: 'Proteção por morte',
  },
  options: {
    eyebrow: 'A Partir Daí',
    title: 'Duas Opções',
    opt1: 'Opção 1 · Resgatar',
    opt1Body: 'Retirar o valor acumulado para usar como quiser.',
    opt2: 'Opção 2 · Deixar rendendo',
    incomeForLife: 'Renda projetada por toda a vida',
    illustratedToAge: (age) => ` (ilustrada até os ${age} anos)`,
    perYear: '/ano',
    none: '—',
  },
  comparison: {
    eyebrow: 'Comparando os Produtos',
    title: 'Termo vs. IUL',
    term: 'Seguro Temporário',
    iul: 'IUL ★',
    rows: [
      { label: 'Duração', term: '20 anos, depois expira', iul: 'Vitalícia, sem expiração' },
      { label: 'Valor acumulado', term: 'Não possui', iul: 'Sim, cresce ao longo do tempo' },
      { label: 'Mensalidade', term: 'Fixa, mais baixa', iul: 'Flexível, pode aumentar' },
      { label: 'Melhor para', term: 'Proteção máxima a baixo custo, por um período', iul: 'Proteção vitalícia + patrimônio' },
    ],
  },
  disclaimers: { eyebrow: 'Informações Importantes', title: 'Avisos legais' },
  headline: {
    eyebrow: 'Três Formas de Proteger',
    title: 'O que este plano faz por você',
    whenEarly: 'Se você partir cedo',
    whenLong: 'Se você viver muito',
    whenIll: 'Se você adoecer',
    labelDeath: 'Proteção por Morte',
    labelIncome: 'Renda vitalícia',
    labelLiving: 'Benefícios em vida',
    subDeath: 'Pago à sua família, livre de imposto de renda',
    illustratedToAge: (age) => `ilustrada até os ${age} anos`,
    livingUpToFull: 'até 100% do benefício',
    livingEarly: 'Acesso antecipado ao benefício',
    livingUpTo: (money) => `até ${money}`,
  },
  clientFallback: 'o cliente',
}

const en: SlideCopy = {
  cover: { subtitle: 'Personalized Proposal', clientFallback: 'Client', agentFallback: 'Agent' },
  explainer: {
